title: Dubai Day Trips from Abu Dhabi
description:
You might want to take a day trip to Dubai from Abu Dhabi to experience its world-famous landmarks like the Burj Khalifa and the Dubai Mall. 
The city offers unique attractions such as indoor skiing at Ski Dubai and the Dubai Fountain's light and water show. 
Additionally, you can enjoy different culinary experiences with a wide variety of international cuisine.
tagline: See the modern metropolis of Dubai for a day

title: Al Ain Day Trips from Abu Dhabi
description: Discover the cultural and historical treasures of Al Ain on a day trip from Abu Dhabi. Explore the lush Al Ain Oasis, visit the Al Jahili Fort, and experience the vibrant local markets.
tagline: Visit Al Ain Oasis and shop at the local markets

title: Abu Dhabi Hop-on Hop-off Tours
description: Explore Abu Dhabi at your own pace, visiting top attractions like the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Emirates Palace, and the Louvre Abu Dhabi. A flexible tour allows you to see the city's highlights without the hassle of navigating on your own.
tagline: Visit top landmarks like Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque and the Louvre Abu Dhabi with ease

title: The Butterfly Gardens Abu Dhabi
description: Observe numerous species of butterflies in their natural habitat. Walk through beautifully landscaped gardens filled with colorful flowers.
tagline: Watch butterflies and learn about their life cycle

title: Al Noor Island
description: Walk across the bridge and enjoy the views. Visit the butterfly house and see different species.
tagline: Walk through the butterfly house and visit the literature pavilion

title: Pearls Kingdom Waterpark
description:
Discover a world where daring pirates, elusive treasures, mystical carpets, opulent palaces, and hidden galaxies await. Welcome to Pearl's Kingdom, a family-friendly destination filled with magic and excitement.
Ranked as the 7th best water park in the world, this premier venue features over 30 aquatic rides and slides, ensuring fun for all ages.
Located in Sharjah, known for its historical significance and vibrant culture, Pearl's Kingdom is just a 20-minute drive from both Dubai and Sharjah Airports, making it an easily accessible escape.
tagline: Slide down water slides and relax in the lazy river

title: Central Souq
description: The Central Souq in Sharjah was built in 1978. It's such an iconic feature of the UAE that they put it on the Dh5 note! It's located between King Faisal Road and Khalid Lagoon.

title: Sharjah Fort
description: Sharjah Fort is a historic fortification in the center of Sharjah, in the United Arab Emirates. The fort was partially destroyed during the 1970s, but has since been faithfully restored.

title: Day Trips from Sharjah
description:
A day trip from Sharjah offers you a chance to visit bustling markets and the nearby coastline. 
You can explore the Sharjah Aquarium to see marine life up close. For a bit of history, the Sharjah Museum of Islamic Civilization provides fascinating exhibits and artifacts.
tagline: Explore the history, culture and landscapes of the UAE

title: Sharjah Desert Safari
description: Sharjah Desert Safari is an opportunity for you to step away from the hustle and bustle of city life and experience the tranquility of the desert. You'll get a chance to see wildlife in their natural habitat, ride a camel, and sleep under the stars in a traditional Bedouin tent. The desert's silence
tagline: Spot falcons and ride camels under the desert sun

title: Sharjah Hop-on Hop-off Tours
description: Visit famous landmarks and historical sites. Enjoy the convenience of a flexible schedule to explore the city's attractions at your own pace.
tagline: Visit museums and ride along the waterfront

title: Sharjah Boat Tours
description: Take a boat ride along the coast and view the city skyline from the water. Spot various marine life and enjoy the coastal scenery.
tagline: Cruise through the canals and view traditional architecture

title: Island Of Legends
description: Visit historical sites and learn about the local culture. Enjoy water activities like snorkeling and sailing.
tagline: Visit ancient ruins and ride the famous riverboat

title: Outdoor Activities in Sharjah
description: Visit the natural reserves and observe diverse bird species. Ride a bike along the coastal paths and enjoy the amazing views.
tagline: Ride a dune buggy and try sandboarding

title: Sagrada Familia
description:
The Sagrada Familia is the magnum opus of the famous Catalan architect Antoni Gaudí. The cathedral was the finale of his incredible Modernist career and has been under continuous construction since 1882. 
Over time, the so-called 'architect of God' dropped all his other work so that he could focus exclusively on this project, which he did until his untimely death in 1926.
The Sagrada Familia is due to be finished in 2026, on the 100th anniversary of Gaudí's death. By that time, the construction of the Sagrada Familia will have taken 144 years.
tagline: Get your Sagrada Familia tickets to see inside Gaudí's crowning masterpiece in Barcelona

title: Park Güell
description: Park Güell is Gaudí's fantasy-fuelled park in Carmel Hill, Barcelona. Walk amongst fantastic creations built in Gaudí's special brand of Catalan Modernism and enjoy panoramic views of Barcelona.
tagline: Secure your Park Güell tickets and enter into Gaudí’s colorful world with timed entry that lets you see it all

title: Casa Batlló
description: Gaudí's masterpiece, Casa Batlló located in the heart of Barcelona, was built in 1877. In 1904, the wealthy Batlló family asked Gaudí to build another in its place. Instead, he decided to renovate the building and did so in his own inimitable style. With a wavy animalistic facade, brilliant colors, and thought-provoking whimsical features everywhere (check out the roof terrace), this is a Gaudí wonder.
tagline: Get your Casa Batlló tickets and experience Barcelona's House of Bones and it’s audio visual rooms with timed-entry

title: Montjuïc Cable Car
description:
Enjoy panoramic views of Barcelona by taking a recently-refurbished Montjuïc cable car to Parc Montjuïc. 
This 750-meter-long cable car connects Barcelona to the top of a mountain, where you'll have the very best views of the Catalan capital and the beach below.
tagline: Elevate your Barcelona experience with a cable car ride to the top of Parc Montjuïc

title: Barcelona Hop-on Hop-off Tours
description:
Ditch the walking tours and set itineraries and explore Barcelona your way with these city bus tour selections. 
Get to know Barcelona the easy way with a convenient hop-on hop-off bus tour around the city. See Barcelona's best sights at your own pace with guided narration in multiple languages onboard. 
Speed over to Sagrada Familia, cruise to Casa Mila, and check out Plaça de Catalunya (and so much more) all on the same day with a hop-on hop-off option.
Looking to beat the heat (and the crowds)? Opt for a night bus tour around Barcelona to see the city in a different light. Or, pick your own route with a public transport card (and save your soles).
tagline: Explore all the highlights of Barcelona at your own pace

title: Barcelona Aquarium
description:
A world-class aquarium located in the heart of Barcelona, Spain, showcasing a stunning collection of marine life from around the world. 
With over 11,000 animals representing 450 species, visitors can explore a variety of exhibits, including a tropical ocean tank, a Mediterranean reef display, and a shark tunnel. 
The aquarium offers educational programs, interactive exhibits, and conservation initiatives to raise awareness about protecting our oceans.
tagline: See over 11,000 marine wonders in Barcelona's premier aquarium

title: Casa Milà - La Pedrera
description:
Another masterpiece by Gaudí built from 1905-10, commissioned by the wealthy Pere Milà. This building was designed to be used as a fashionable home for the Milà family and as an office block.
The house's original name was Casa Milà; however, the building is better known by its name "La Pedrera" (The Quarry), which refers to the uneven gray stone facade that ripples around the corner of Carrer de Provença.
tagline: Discover Gaudí's architectural brilliance in Barcelona

title: Barcelona Self-Guided Tours
description:
Embark on an unforgettable journey through Barcelona with Barcelona Self-Guided Tours. Discover the enchanting beauty and rich history of this vibrant city at your own pace. 
Our self-guided tours offer the freedom to explore Barcelona's iconic landmarks, hidden gems, and local treasures. Immerse yourself in the captivating architecture of Gaudí, wander through charming neighborhoods, savor delicious cuisine, and soak up the Mediterranean vibes. 
With Barcelona Self-Guided Tours, you have the flexibility to create your own itinerary and delve into the heart and soul of Barcelona. Start your adventure today!
tagline: Unveil Barcelona's Secrets with Self-Guided Tours!

title: Spotify Camp Nou & FC Barcelona Museum
description: Tour the Spotify Camp Nou stadium and the amazing  FC Barcelona Museum. Immersive and impressive, the museum features giant touch screens, sound installations (including match-day sounds), historical stats, and a trophy cabinet worthy of King Midas!
tagline: Visit Camp Nou and relive FC Barcelona's greatest moments

title: Barcelona: Public Transport Tickets
description:
Explore the amazing city of Barcelona with ease and convenience using the public transport system. With a vast range of buses, trams, and metro lines, you'll be able to get to all the incredible attractions, destinations and neighborhoods that make Barcelona such a unique city. 
Visit the beach, explore the historic city center, and take a day trip out of town. Buy your tickets today and explore Barcelona like a local.
tagline: Get around Barcelona with ease using public transport

title: Barcelona Airport Transfer
description: Ride to your hotel or other destination from Barcelona Airport. Enjoy a hassle-free transfer experience with professional services and drivers.
tagline: Meet your ride for easy transfer to your destination

title: PortAventura World
description:
PortAventura World is a family-friendly entertainment resort that includes three spectacular parks: PortAventura Park, Ferrari Land, and Caribe Aquatic Park. 
Ride one (or all) of 40 roller coasters at PortAventura Park, speed around the racetrack at the F1-themed park in Europe at Ferrari Land, and cool down during the Spanish summer with dozens of water rides at Caribe Aquatic Park.
tagline: Get your thrills (or chill) at one of Spain's top spots – be sure you don't miss Ferrari Land!

title: Barcelona Zoo
description: Barcelona Zoo is a place where you can meet animals from around the world in carefully designed habitats. Whether it's monkeys in the trees or fish in underwater tanks, there's plenty to see. The zoo also provides play areas for kids, complete with an array of activities that promote learning about animals. Alongside all the fun, the zoo is also deeply involved in international conservation projects, offering a chance for you to learn about these important efforts.
tagline: Explore animal habitats and enjoy 5,500 animals and 400+ species at this impressive city zoo!

title: Casa Vicens
description: See the beginning of Antoni Gaudí's incredible architectural legacy at this immaculately designed summer house in the heart of Barcelona.
tagline: Tour the rooms and visit the garden

title: Recinte Modernista de Sant Pau
description:
Recinte Modernista de Sant Pau is the masterpiece of architect Lluís Domènech i Montaner, an avant-garde of the Catalan Modernisme movement. In the early 1900s, he envisioned a hospital that would not just heal the bodies of patients, but also their spirits.
The resulting Sant Pau Hospital is an Art Nouveau complex boasting 16 pavilions, tranquil gardens, mosaic art, domed ceilings, playful pastel walls, and polychromatic tiles. The building feels less like a hospital and more like a surreal place of healing. Underground tunnels that connect the individual buildings are also open to visitors.
tagline: Discover a hidden gem of Modernisme architecture in Barcelona

title: Moco Museum Barcelona
description: View contemporary and modern art collections. Explore digital art installations and interactive exhibits.
tagline: View contemporary art and interactive installations

title: Barcelona City Cards
description:
There’s not many places that have been so molded by one man in the way Antoni Gaudí sculpted the architecture of Barcelona. 
A Barcelona city card secures your spot at famous venues while also sparing you from the long lines at Sagrada Familia and Park Güell.
This quick and convenient solutions allow you to see the best of his architecture, inside and out, while also giving you a number of other perks – access to public transport to shuttle you around the city and discounted entry to top art museums, like the Museu Picasso of Barcelona, are often included in a city card.
tagline: See some of Barcelona's top local landmarks and attractions with easy and convenient City Cards

title: Palau de la Música Catalana
description: The Palau de la Música Catalana is a luxurious Catalan Art Nouveau masterpiece. Built between 1905 and 1908, the building unites the decorative arts of sculpture, mosaic, stained glass, and ironwork. Surprisingly it wasn't designed by Gaudí! It was dreamed up by another Catalan architect, Lluís Domènech i Montaner. The Palau was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997.
tagline: Witness the beauty of Barcelona's UNESCO-listed concert hall

title: Montserrat Day Trips from Barcelona
description: Montserrat Day Trips from Barcelona offer a unique opportunity to explore the stunning natural beauty of Montserrat Mountain and its famous Benedictine monastery. Visitors can enjoy breathtaking views of the Catalan countryside, visit the Black Madonna, and listen to the world-renowned Escolania boys' choir. The traditional trip usually includes transportation, a guided tour, and free time to explore the area. It is a perfect way to escape the city and experience the rich cultural heritage of Catalonia.
tagline: Explore stunning landscapes and learn about Montserrat's rich heritage on a day trip from Barcelona

title: Palau Güell (Güell Palace)
description:
Palau Güell was one of the first important commissions Antoni Gaudí received at the start of his career. Eusebi Güell (industrialist, politician, and patron of the arts) wanted Gaudí to build him this peculiar urban palace as an extension of the family home on La Rambla. 
Today, you can book Palau Güell tickets online and visit a palace that's considered one of the masterpieces of the city's architectural landscape.

title: Palau Martorell
description:
Palau Martorell is an exhibition center in the heart of Barcelona's cultural scene. It showcases works by famous artists like Chagall, Alphonse Mucha, and Sorolla. Future exhibitions will feature Calder, Basquiat, and Tamara de Lempicka. 
The venue hosts international exhibitions, family activities, contemporary artist programs, and various lectures and conferences.
tagline: Visit the exhibition center in Barcelona showcasing international exhibitions

title: Barcelona Cable Car (Port Cable Car)
description:
If you're really looking to elevate your Barcelona adventure, look no further than the Barcelona Port Cable Car. This aerial tramway soars high above the 'Modernisme' architecture and bustling streets of the Catalan capital, providing a splendid bird's-eye view of the city. 
The Port Cable Car crosses the historic harbor of Port Vell and connects the Montjuïc hill with the seaside suburb of Barceloneta. It's not to be confused with the Montjuïc Cable Car, which links the upper terminus of the Montjuïc funicular with Montjuïc Castle.
tagline: Ride over the city and visit Montjuïc Castle

title: Poble Espanyol
description:
Poble Espanyol is a museum that feels like an actual village. Built in 1929 for a world exhibition, it’s a lifesize replica of a Spanish town that features building styles from all regions of Spain. There are 117 buildings in total, and today these make up a beautiful cluster of restaurants and workshops that reflect the many diverse lifestyles, cuisines, and cultures of traditional Spain.
Take a nice stroll through, have some tapas and peruse the Spanish arts and crafts shops.
tagline: Explore Barcelona at your own pace, including its architecture, culture, and local crafts

title: IDEAL Montjuïc
description:
IDEAL Montjuïc is focused on immersive exhibitions and digital culture. The venue features large projection spaces, virtual reality experiences, and interactive installations.
Visitors can explore exhibitions that combine art, technology, and storytelling in an engaging and accessible way.
tagline: Explore ancient artifacts and see digital recreations of tombs

title: Tibidabo Amusement Park
description: Ride the Ferris wheel and get a view of the city. Enjoy the vintage carousels and classic amusement park rides.
tagline: Ride the Ferris wheel and visit the old-fashioned carousel

title: Fundació Joan Miró
description:
The hometown museum of one of the 20th century's greatest artists is the outcome of a friendship between two extraordinary creative personalities: Joan Miró, and his friend, the architect Josep Lluís Sert. The Fundació Joan Miró not only houses the single greatest collection of his work – the unconventional building is considered one of the most outstanding museum buildings in the world.
Once you step inside, you’ll discover the paintings, sculptures, some textiles and drawings. There are thousands of items here and you’ll be able to pick out the artistic evolution of the artist.
tagline: View Miró’s artwork and visit the sculpture garden

title: La Roca Village Transfers from Barcelona
description: La Roca Village is a boutique outlet shopping village, located just 40 min from Barcelona city centre. Featuring a dizzying array of designer brand outlets and amazing prices all-year-round, La Roca Village provides a perfect mix of retail therapy for fashionistas and savvy bargain hunters alike. The tranquil tree-lined setting and slew of trendy cafes and restaurants also make it an ideal place to chill for an afternoon while you shop till you drop!

title: Barcelona Boat Trips
description:
Explore Barcelona's stunning coastline and take in the city's iconic skyline on a boat trip. 
See the city from the sea and pass by some of Barcelona's most iconic landmarks on a leisurely cruise with breathtaking views.
With a Barcelona boat trip, you'll get to experience the Catalan capital's beauty like never before. Don't miss out on an incredible sightseeing opportunity!
tagline: Witness Barcelona's beauty like never before

title: Barcelona Cathedral
description: With its intricate Gothic façade and rich history dating back to the 13th century, this awe-inspiring cathedral is a symbol of Catalan heritage and religious devotion.
tagline: Discover the rich history dating back to the 13th century

title: Montserrat Mountain
description: Hike through scenic trails and enjoy the natural landscapes. Visit the monastery and explore its historical significance.
tagline: Hike the trails, visit the monastery, and enjoy amazing views

title: Montserrat Cable Car Station
description: Ready to get the most awe-inspiring mountain views ever? The Montserrat Cable Car Station in Barcelona, Spain, entered into service in 1930, and is still the starting point of the mountain's dedicated cable car. Once you've flashed your ticket, board at the station and take the swift skyward journey towards the popular Santa Maria de Montserrat Abbey.

title: Museu Nacional d’Art de Catalunya
description:
The Museu Nacional D’art de Catalunya is the home of the world’s finest collection of Romanesque mural paintings and lots more by the greats, including Gaudi! 
The museum is located in Barcelona and is easy to reach by subway.

title: Gaudí Experience
description:
The Gaudí Experience is one of Barcelona's top attractions, giving visitors the chance to get acquainted with the Modernist architect's work in a 4D experience and exhibition. 
Located close to the iconic Park Güell, this interactive journey is also close to the Lesseps metro stop.

title: Museu Marítim de Barcelona
description: View historic ships and maritime artifacts. Learn about the region's naval history through interactive exhibits.
tagline: View ancient ships and learn about maritime history

title: Flamenco Barcelona City Hall Theater
description:
Flamenco Theatre, City Hall Barcelona has been recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage site since 2010 and has also, for the fourth consecutive year, won the Travelers Choice award, making it among the 10% of the best attractions in the world. 
Dedicated to introducing the art form of Flamenco to those less acquainted with it, spreading the passion of the local dance, and hosting an array of talented performers, singers, and musicians, the Flamenco Theatre City Hall in Barcelona is the perfect place to go for an authentic Flamenco show.
tagline: The place where Flamenco breathes and beats

title: Mies van der Rohe Pavilion
description:
The Mies van der Rohe Pavilion, a symbolic work of the Modern movement, was designed by Ludwig Mies van der Rohe and Lilly Reich as Germany's national pavilion for the 1929 Barcelona International Exposition. 
After the exhibition closed, the Pavilion was dismantled in 1930. Over time, it became a key reference for Mies van der Rohe's trajectory and for the architecture of the 20th century as a whole.
tagline: Visit the modern architecture and view the reflecting pools

title: The Dalmases Palace
description:
The Dalmases Palace is located in the heart of Barcelona and makes for a toe-tapping addition to any night in the city. The venue has been restored to give it a more modern and sophisticated atmosphere without losing the charm of the original construction, which dates back to the 17th century. It's home to traditional tablao flamenco shows by a medley of world-renowned performers.
Flamenco shows take place every day at 18:00, 19:15, 20:15 and 21:15.

title: White Rabbit Immersive Experience Barcelona
description: Explore Barcelona's unique culture at White Rabbit. Feel Catalonia's vibrant spirit through 10 different installations, including sculpture, virtual reality, music, video dance, graffiti, an immersive cube, a mechatronic installation, and an infinity room.
tagline: Discover Barcelona's traditions at the city's #1 immersive experience

title: Tibidabo Panorama Area
description:
Visit the Tibidabo Panoramic Area, the highest part of the Park, offering free access and the best views of Barcelona. Situated 500 meters above sea level, you can take in the cityscape and get the best views of its iconic attractions and restaurants.
The Panoramic Area is open every day and can be reached with the Cuca de Llum (from Plaça Doctor Andreu). Tibidabo is the highest peak in the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, providing one of the best viewpoints to admire the city.
tagline: Visit the highest peak in Barcelona, offering the best viewpoints

title: Paradox Museum Barcelona
description:
With more than 70 amazing exhibitions inspired by paradoxes, Paradox Museum Barcelona is the perfect place to experiment with all the senses, which invites you to ask questions about the world around us, to see reality through altered perceptions and to discover fascinating scientific concepts. 
Follow the signs and discover the exhibits in a space designed to discover them in a one-way flow. Take amazing photos and videos, enjoy the descriptions of the exhibits that explain “what to do” and “what's going on”.
Scan the QR codes to learn even more and feel free to ask questions of the team members. They can even take group photos of you. Before returning to reality, visit the Paradox Boutique for a unique souvenir.
tagline: Enter a world where nothing makes sense, yet everything seems real, and let paradoxes redefine your reality

title: Illa Fantasia Waterpark
description:
Make a splash at Illa Fantasia Waterpark – one of the best places for watery family fun in Spain. The park has more than 22 action-packed water rides guaranteed to beat the boredom (and the heat).
Illa Fantasia Waterpark is within easy reach of Barcelona by train or car.
tagline: Slide down water rides and swim in large pools

title: Mirador Torre Glòries
description:
The Mirador Torre Glòries (Glories Tower) is an iconic landmark of Barcelona designed by Jean Nouvel and inaugurated in 2007. 
In 2022, Torre Glòries opened a lookout on its 30th floor, becoming the highest viewpoint in the city. The lookout combines art and architecture in an immersive experience that is sure to become one of the city highlights.
tagline: Get the best views of Barcelona from the 30th floor

title: Wax Museum Barcelona
description: Just off Las Ramblas, Barcelona’s Wax Museum (Museu de Cera) has more than 150 famous historical characters cast in wax. The museum is tucked into a little alley in a stunning 19th-century Neoclassical building. The museum and its collection were renovated recently, so keep an eye out for the latest figures, like Greta Thunberg!

title: Codorníu Winery
description:
As soon as you arrive at the tall gates of Codorníu Winery, you'll get a sense of its heritage and tradition.
Since the 16th century, the family-run Codorníu business has been producing premium wines in Spain. From the late 20th century onwards, it became known for one of the world's best loved sparkling tipples: cava.
Through a guided tour of the winery, you'll learn the history of winemaking in the area, some of the secrets to cava's subtle flavors, and, of course, enjoy a tasting of their finest wares with an expert.
tagline: Tour the cellars and taste the sparkling wines

title: Tibidabo Funicular
description: Ride to the top and enjoy the amusement park. Take in the view of the city from the observation deck.
tagline: Ride the funicular and visit an amusement park

title: RCD Espanyol Stadium
description: The RCDE Stadium is home to RCD Espanyol de Barcelona, a club with 120+ years of history. A tour of this spectacular stadium will give you a deeper insight into the ins and outs of the club, and take you through facilities including the press room, the first team dressing room, as well as a historical room featuring trophies and jerseys from all eras. Live out your football fantasies and pretend to be a legendary coach, player, or club president!

title: Picasso Museum
description: Step into the five delightful medieval palaces that house the Picasso Museum, and begin an exploration of the 20th century’s greatest artist. In all, there are over 3,500 works by Picasso; while the collection focuses mainly on his formative creative years, you’ll also see works from throughout his career to get a full sense of this visionary's evolution.
tagline: Witness over 3,500 works by Pablo Picasso, all under one roof

title: Casa Amatller
description:
Casa Amatller is an eye-catching building designed in the Modernisme style by Josep Puig i Cadafalch. 
It features an array of fantastical busts and reliefs of dragons, knights and more on the outside. On the inside visitors can wander the rooms full of furniture from past resident, well-known businessman and chocolatier Antoni Amatller.

title: Costa Brava Boat Trips
description: Explore the beauty of Costa Brava on a romantic rose boat tour! Enjoy the stunning coastline, the sunshine, and the crystal-clear waters of the Mediterranean Sea. You and your loved one will have the perfect opportunity to create a lifetime of memories together. Relax and admire the incredible views and fresh air as you drift along the water. This boat tour is a must for anyone wanting to experience the beauty of Costa Brava!
tagline: Experience the beauty of Costa Brava on a romantic rose boat tour!

title: Banksy Museum Barcelona
description: View famous street art by Banksy and learn about its impact. Attend exhibits showcasing various works and styles.
tagline: See murals, scenography, and video elements

title: Torre Bellesguard
description: The Torre Bellesguard is a lesser-known Gaudi attraction, hidden up in the hills of Barcelona. This castle-inspired neo-Gothic house later went on to serve as a foundation for some of Gaudi's work on the Sagrada Familia. Plus, visiting Torre Bellesguard is a great excuse to visit the Sarrià-Sant Gervasi area of the city.

title: Colònia Güell
description: Colònia Güell is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Crypt was built by Gaudi as a place of worship for the people in this manufacturing suburb, under the instruction of entrepreneur Eusebio Güell.

title: Tablao Flamenco Cordobes
description: Watch traditional flamenco performances by skilled dancers. Enjoy authentic Spanish cuisine while listening to live music.
tagline: Watch flamenco shows and taste traditional Spanish food

title: Museu de la Xocolata de Barcelona
description: You'll find The Chocolate Museum in the former Saint Agustí monastery, located at Carrer Comerç, 36, 08003 Barcelona. Once inside, you'll be on a tour of the origins of chocolate and here in this impressive space you'll learn about chocolate's arrival in Europe, the way it was revered for both medicinal and nutritional properties, and get to sample the goods, too.

title: Catalunya en Miniatura
description: This miniature park is anything but small! Spanning more than 60,000 square meters, it contains some 147 painstakingly realized models of Catalonia's most iconic buildings – recreated at 1:33 scale. There's also a fully fledged adventure part, allowing intrepid visitors of all ages to leap, slide and swing around the treetop course!

title: House of Candy Immersive Experience Barcelona
description: Find a wide variety of candies and sweets. Taste different flavors and enjoy the colorful displays.
tagline: Enjoy a multisensory experience that celebrates the sweetest side of life

title: Columbus Monument
description: At the base of Barcelona's famous La Rambla stands this 60 m tall monument dedicated to Christopher Columbus (known in Catalan as Monument a Colom). Built in 1888 for Barcelona's Universal Exposition this monument was designed to let the world know that Christopher Columbus reported back to the Spanish Royal Family after he 'discovered' America.
tagline: Trace the history of Christopher Columbus in Barcelona

title: IKONO Barcelona (Arenas de Barcelona Shopping Centre)
description:
IKONO Barcelona is a immersive experience showcasing interactive art installations where you become the subject. 
Get lost in surreal and incredible scenarios and capture it all on camera at IKONO Barcelona.
tagline: Immerse yourself in incredible art installations at IKONO Barcelona

title: European Museum of Modern Art
description:
The European Museum of Modern Art (MEAM) is based in an old palace that was rebuilt in 1791 by a textile merchant in the La Ribera neighborhood, very close to the Picasso Museum in Barcelona.
When, in the mid-nineteenth century, Calle de la Princesa was opened to connect the old town with the Castell dels Tres Dragons in Ciutadella Park, the palace was divided into two parts, on both sides of the street.
The MEAM presents its own collections, mostly of figurative and realistic works, structured in three categories: contemporary art of the 21st century, modern sculpture of the 20th century, and Catalan sculpture of the 20th century, as well as other temporary exhibitions.

title: Erotic Museum of Barcelona
description: The Erotic Museum of Barcelona is a sex museum located close to the Mercado de la Boquería in Barcelona, Spain. It offers visitors insight into erotica of the past through art and exhibits.

title: Barcelona Water Sports
description:
Experience Barcelona on the water and book your spot on an exciting aquatic activity. 
Hop on a jet ski and ride some waves, or hold on tight as you and your friends bounce around on a banana boat. Wit the Mediterranean sea so close, don't miss your chance to make the most of it. 
With crystal blue waters and stunning views of the city, you'll have one of the most unique experiences in Barcelona. Whether it's your first time discovering water sports, or you're a seasoned pro – there's something for everyone.
tagline: Start a new adventure in Barcelona with an exciting water sports experience

title: Barcelona Bike Tours
description:
Explore Barcelona on two wheels and experience the city like a local! 
These bike tours are the best way to get to know the city, with experienced guides to lead the way. Enjoy the sun and the breeze as you cycle past beautiful architecture, breathtaking views, and bustling markets. 
Get ready to discover hidden gems and popular hotspots as you cycle through the Catalan capital.
tagline: Ride through Barcelona and make unforgettable memories

title: Barcelona City Tours
description:
Explore Barcelona with a fun and exciting city tour! See the city's stunning architecture, lively culture, and beautiful scenery. 
Enjoy a guided tour of the city and get to know its vibrant neighborhoods. After meeting up with a friendly guide, they'll show you the best of Barcelona and help you make the most of your time in the city! 
Take your pick from a range of different tours that showcase the most interesting areas in Barcelona, and decide how you do it – by foot, by bus, by bike, or even by tuk-tuk!
tagline: Get to know Barcelona on an interesting tour of the city

title: Los Tarantos Barcelona
description:
The Tarantos Flamenco show takes place at Los Tarantos - the oldest flamenco tablao of Barcelona, founded in 1963. 
Located in the city center, close to Las Ramblas, Los Tarantos is at Plaça Reial 17 in the Ciutat Vella quarter of the city. It's close to lots of attractions, bars, and restaurants.

title: Freixenet Winery
description: Take a guided tour of the winemaking facilities. Sample different varieties of sparkling wine.
tagline: Taste sparkling wine and tour the underground cellars

title: Cardona Salt Mountain
description: One of the largest and oldest-such geological formations in the world, Cardona Salt Mountain is a cultural gem of Catalunya which was formed over two million years ago as dense rock pushed salt towards the Earth's surface. This mountain of salt was discovered by the ancient Romans and has been bolstering the local economy ever since. These days, you can plumb the cavernous salt mines with traditional guided tours and theatrical tours that cover the amazing history of the site.

title: Barcelona Segway Tours
description:
Experience the thrill of gliding through the vibrant streets of Barcelona on a Segway tour! Get ready to explore this enchanting European city the easy way. 
Feel the wind in your hair as you swoop around the lively squares and admire the stunning architecture. Let your knowledgeable guide show you the best of Barcelona and its hidden gems.
Don't worry about learning to ride it, you'll have safety gear provided and plenty of instructions before you start the tour.
tagline: Explore Barcelona with an unforgettable segway adventure

title: Castle of Cardona
description:
The Castle of Cardona was constructed in 886 and is considered one of the most important medieval castles in all of Spain. Sitting majestically on a hilltop, it offers eye-melting views of the surrounding valley and the Pyrenees. 
The building has undergone numerous renovations and extensions over the course of the centuries. You'll see elements of Romanesque and Gothic styles, plus the Minyona tower, where the young maid Adalés was allegedly kept as a recluse by her parents for being involved in an illicit affair!
Visitors can also explore the Church of Sant Vicenç, built in 1040 on the site of the old chapel of the castle. Don't miss the crypt below the presbytery – the Chapel of the Relics.

title: Castell de Montjuïc
description:
In a comfortable small group and with your professional guide take the Montjuic funicular and get ready for the best views of Barcelona from top. Enjoy the breathtaking views from the Montjuic Cable Car and  wonderful views of Barcelona’s iconic skyline.
Reach the Montjuic Castle to enjoy a private visit allowed only to our small group. The Montjuic Castle has been a military fortress since its early beginning, witnessing the change of political power in the city. 
We invite you to an exclusive visit, not opened to general public, where you will be able to enter the dungeons of the several prisoners that have marked the history of the city.
Arise from the dungeons to the sky to admire a bird’s eye view of Barcelona and its surroundings. 
The best way to admire Barcelona in all its beauty is from high above the city. This is where Barcelona reveals itself in total splendour. With our exclusive visit, you will have access to the Tower , where you can admire a 360º views of Barcelona

title: Barcelona Museum of Contemporary Art
description: The permanent collection at MACBA boasts around 5,000 works from the mid-20th century onwards.  There's enough mind-blowing art inside to keep you busy for hours. MACBA is located in is Plaça dels Angels, close to two Metro stations.

title: Egyptian Museum of Barcelona
description: First opened in 1994, the Museo Egipcio de Barcelona (Egyptian Museum of Barcelona) is a museum of Egyptology located on Carrer de València, in Barcelona, Spain. It was the first museum of its kind in Spain, and exhibits over 1,100 artifacts from ancient Egypt in its permanent and temporary exhibitions.

title: Teatro Flamenco Barcelona
description: Watch live flamenco performances featuring skilled dancers and musicians. Enjoy an authentic cultural experience with traditional Spanish music and dance.
tagline: Watch a flamenco show and listen to live Spanish guitar music

title: ECLIPSO Barcelona
description: Explore a detailed recreation of the Titanic's interiors. Interact with exhibits that explain the ship's history and its tragic voyage.
tagline: Explore the ship's deck and interact with historical artifacts

title: Barcelona Flamenco Shows
description:
Experience an evening of passion, grace, and fiery emotion with a flamenco show in Barcelona! 
Lose yourself in the captivating rhythms and vibrant energy of this traditional Spanish art form. Watch talented dancers move to the music, feel the energy of the passionate performance, and be amazed by the skill involved. 
Choose from a range of flamenco shows and enjoy the captivating beauty of flamenco during your stay in Barcelona!
tagline: Experience the fiery emotion and graceful beauty of flamenco in Barcelona

title: Museum of Illusions Barcelona
description: Home to some of the quirkiest selfie backdrops in Barcelona, the Museum of Illusions is the perfect place to spend an hour of silly fun between tapas crawls and Barcelona's more highbrow cultural offerings. Who wouldn't want a selfie with the open jaws of a hungry T. rex?
tagline: Solve puzzles and interact with optical illusions

title: Barcelona Bus Transfers
description: Take a comfortable ride to various destinations around the city. Enjoy convenient and timely service to reach key attractions and points of interest.
tagline: Visit famous landmarks and ride comfortable buses

title: Barcelona Helicopter Flights
description:
See Barcelona from a brand-new perspective with an exhilarating helicopter flight! 
Soar above the city, admiring stunning cityscape views and taking in the vibrant atmosphere from up high. 
Experience the best of Barcelona with a memorable journey that will stay with you for a lifetime! With comfortable seating and the latest safety features, you can sit back and relax as you enjoy the ride. Don't miss out on this truly unique opportunity.
tagline: Take flight and experience Barcelona from a whole new perspective!

title: Barcelona Synagogue
description: This Jewish religious building in Barcelona is one of the oldest in Europe. Learn about the life of the Jewish citizens of Barcelona before the Spanish Inquisition, their expulsion from the country, and their contributions to the culture of Catalonia.
tagline: Discover the history of Barcelona's Jewish citizens at one of the oldest synagogues in Europe

title: Reial Cercle Artístic de Barcelona
description: View diverse art exhibitions and appreciate the works of local artists. Attend workshops and participate in creative activities.
tagline: Visit the art exhibitions and attend a live performance

title: Barcelona Locker Rentals
description:
Are you looking for a convenient and secure place to store your belongings while exploring Barcelona? Look no further! 
These Barcelona locker rental options provide the perfect storage solution for your belongings while you go sightseeing, shopping, and more. 
With easy access, you'll have a safe and secure place to store your important items, so you can enjoy your day without worrying about your belongings.
tagline: Get secure storage for your belongings and explore Barcelona with peace of mind

title: Big Fun Museum
description: The Big Fun Museum is an interactive exhibition located in Barcelona, Spain. Opened in 2018, it contains nine small museums dedicated to a range of colorful themes, including an upside down house and a museum of illusions.
tagline: Walk through upside-down rooms and take photos with 3D illusions

title: Barcelona Food Tours
description:
Explore the world-famous cuisine of Barcelona on a delicious food tour! 
You'll get an authentic taste of the city's culinary offerings from traditional tapas to local delicacies. Savor the flavors of Spain's second-largest city and learn about its history and culture from a friendly local guide.
tagline: Taste Barcelona's tapas and local cuisine

title: Museu d'Història de Catalunya
description: The Museu d'Història de Catalunya is a museum of Catalonian history. It's located close to the port in an atmospheric building that also features an excellent rooftop terrace. It's easy to reach on foot and public transport from most parts of Barcelona.

title: Tàpies Museum
description: The Tàpies Museum is an art gallery dedicated to modern, postmodern, and contemporary art. View a collection of works by Antoni Tàpies. Attend exhibitions and educational programs related to contemporary art.
tagline: Visit art exhibits and view Antoni Tàpies' works

title: Ice Bar Barcelona
description: The only place in Barcelona that maintains a bracing temperature of -5ºC all year round! Icebarcelona is the coolest spot in the Catalan capital and was designed by internationally renowned artists. Located right by Somorrostro beach, it makes for the perfect spot to chill away from the city's scorching sunshine during the hot months.

title: Costa Brava Day Trip from Barcelona
description:
Discover the best of the Costa Brava on a day trip from Barcelona! Enjoy a full day of beautiful beaches, colorful villages, and stunning natural scenery. 
Explore the region's history, culture, and architecture or simply relax and take in the breathtaking views. With round-trip transport, you'll enjoy the ultimate day out without having to worry about the details. 
Don't miss your chance to experience the stunning beauty of the Costa Brava!
tagline: Experience the beauty of the Costa Brava on a stress-free day trip

title: Casa Rocamora
description: The palazzo Casa Rocamora is the former home of collector Manuel Rocamora Vidal, who was born in Barcelona in 1892. The museum houses his collection of Alcora ceramics, paintings, drawings and sculptures from the 19th and 20th centuries as well as a trove of ancient clothing.

title: Olympic and Sports Museum Joan Antoni Samaranch
description: Olympic and Sports Museum Joan Antoni Samaranch is located on Montjuïc, in Barcelona, Spain. The museum details the history of world sport and the Olympic Games, and contains a huge collection of sporting memorabilia.

title: Palau Baró de Quadras
description: The Palau Baró de Quadras is a private house that opens to the public for tours exclusively on Wednesdays. The building was designed by the Catalan Modernist architect Josep Puig i Cadafalch.

title: Andorra Day Trips from Barcelona
description:
Experience the magic of Andorra, amongst towering mountain peaks, with engaging day trips from Barcelona. 
These truly unique journeys offer a fabulous time out, providing a delightful blend of adventure and culture, all framed by Andorra’s exquisite alpine beauty. 
From breathtaking landscapes to vibrant local traditions, every enthusiastic traveler is destined to find something to love - a testament to the charm of this wonderful Pyrenean nation.
tagline: Head to the Pyrenees mountains on a day trip from Barcelona

title: Antarctica Experience
description: See penguins in their natural habitat. Observe the ice formations and glaciers.
tagline: Get as close as you can to Antarctica without leaving Spain

title: Santa Maria del Mar Church
description:
Santa Maria del Mar is a stunning Gothic church located in the heart of Barcelona, Spain. Known for its impressive architecture and rich history, this church has become a must-see attraction for tourists and locals alike!
Construction on Santa Maria del Mar began in the 14th century, and the church was completed in just 55 years - an impressive feat for the time. Its stunning design features soaring vaulted ceilings and intricate stained glass windows, which provide a breathtaking display of light and color.
Over the centuries, Santa Maria del Mar has survived numerous disasters, including fires and earthquakes. Despite these challenges, the church has stood the test of time and remains a symbol of resilience and strength.
Visitors to Santa Maria del Mar can take a guided tour of the church, which provides a deeper understanding of its history and architecture. There are also regular masses and concerts held in the church, making it a vibrant hub of culture and spirituality.

title: Dalí Museum Day Trips from Barcelona
description:
Experience the thrill of a Dalí Museum day trip from Barcelona, a journey that fuses art, surrealism, and unparalleled scenic beauty. 
These day trips provide a riveting escape from the mundane, plunging you into the creative universe of one of the most famous surrealist artists, Salvador Dalí, without venturing far from the vibrant heart of Barcelona. 
Colourful, captivating, and culturally enriching, these escapes to the Dalí Museum promise a memorable splash of the avant-garde amidst the dazzling brilliance of Spanish heritage.
tagline: Art, surrealism, scenic beauty – all within reach of Barcelona

title: Simón Coll Chocolate Factory
description:
Simón Coll Chocolate Factory, or Espai Xocolata, is a chocolate factory in Barcelona. With a remarkable history that dates back as far as 1840, Simón Coll's full-functioning cocoa kingdom provides the best of old and new.
On a guided tour of Simón Coll Chocolate Factory you can check out a range of interactive exhibits, see chocolate being made by in-house chocolate wizards, admire antique machinery, and even try some of Simón Coll's melt-in-the-mouth chocolate.

title: Barcelona Walking Tours
description:
There's some must-sees in Barcelona – the Sagrada Família, Park Güell, Casa Batlló – but aside from Gaudí's architectural gems, there's still so much of the city waiting to be discovered. 
A walking tour of Barcelona is the perfect way to discover the real Barcelona, thanks to the knowledge of the expert guides who accompany you. 
Discover areas like the Gothic Quarter, enjoy the taste of Catalonia with tapas, or get your fill of Picasso as you explore new areas of the city on a Barcelona walking tour.
tagline: Stroll the busy streets while gazing at the famous landmarks of Barcelona

title: KBr Fundación MAPFRE - Barcelona Photo Center
description: KBr Fundación MAPFRE Barcelona is a hub for photographic art in the city. Found in the entertainment hub of Port Olímpic, the Barcelona Photo Center offers an ever-changing program of exhibitions highlighting masters of photography, contemporary photographers, and historical collections from Spain and Catalonia.

title: Costa Brava Day Trips to Barcelona
description:
The sun-soaked shores of Costa Brava are all well and good, but sometimes you just need the inimitable charm and cultural riches of Barcelona. Get ready for a delightful blend of captivating history and lively culture that will make your heart sing. 
Arriving in Barcelona, prepare to be swept off your feet by the city's infectious energy and enchanting architecture. Stroll along Las Ramblas, a bustling promenade filled with street performers, colorful flower stalls, and mouthwatering food vendors. Indulge in some retail therapy at the trendy boutiques of Passeig de Gràcia or lose yourself in the Gothic Quarter's labyrinthine streets, where history whispers from every ancient stone.
Costa Brava day trips to Barcelona are the perfect blend of relaxation and excitement, where you can soak up the sun, immerse yourself in captivating history, and indulge in the vibrant spirit of Catalonia.
tagline: Be swept off your feet by Barcelona's infectious energy and enchanting architecture.

title: Old Estrella Damm Brewery
description: The Estrella Damm Old Brewery brings you the opportunity to discover how everything started more than 140 years ago. Through our tour, you will see the different spaces like the emblematic Kettle Room, the Power Generator Room or the Historical Archive; among others, that will introduce you on how the Damm beers were produced.

title: Fantàstix by Super3
description: Join an immersive adventure through various settings, such as the great forest and a mysterious castle. Meet beloved Super3 characters and enjoy interactive games.
tagline: Participate in an interactive experience and meet your favorite characters

title: Barcelona Museum of Nougat and Chocolate
description:
Museu de la Xocolata or the Museo del Chocolate in Spanish, takes you on a sweet journey through nougat and chocolate making in Agramunt. 
The museum shows how these treats became a big part of the local life. You'll get to see lots of old tools and machines, and even a replica of the Vicens family's 1933 workshop. It's a fun way to learn about a tradition, dating back to 1741, about how artisan nougat is made.
tagline: Discover nougat history, learn about Torrons Vicens, and see a 1933 candy workshop.

title: Rock Museum Barcelona
description:
Explore the history of rock music at the Guitar Legends Hall, a 1000 m2 venue filled with engaging exhibits. 
Travel through different eras of rock, from a blues bar to stadium concerts, a 1960s TV studio, and music festivals. 
Interact with vintage jukeboxes, listen to records in a music store, watch music videos, and hear guitars on display. You can also try playing real guitars.
tagline: Release your inner rock star in Spain

title: Monastery Món Sant Benet
description: Monastery Món Sant Benet is a thousand-year old monastery set in  beautiful mountains. It's part of a larger compound that includes an art nouveau mansion that has been lovingly restored, and is noted for a place of tranquil beauty that marries the very old with modern, simple design.

title: Las Brujas Flamenco Club
description: Watch live flamenco performances by talented dancers. Enjoy authentic Spanish cuisine and drinks while listening to traditional music.
tagline: Watch flamenco dancing and listen to live music

title: Jean Leon Winery
description: Taste a variety of wines crafted from local grapes. Take a guided tour to understand the winemaking process.
tagline: Taste wines and join a guided tour of the vineyard

title: Cal Gerrer House Museum
description: The old Arpí Ceramic Products Factory, commonly known as Cal Gerrer, is the headquarters of the Cabanas Foundation. Now a museum spread across several floors, once you've got up close to the precious pottery, you can learn about the late and great Marilyn Monroe in an exhibition of personal items, photographs, and more. It is located in Sant Cugat del Vallès, Barcelona, Spain.

title: Museu del Còmic i la Il·lustració
description: Barcelona's home of comic book history tells the tale of the graphic novel on a journey across three floors, which spans more than 135 years! Inside you'll find the first story told entirely in a graphic narrative, right through to the birth of DC and Marvel superheroes and the 20th-century pantheon they've created in the modern world.

title: Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum Barcelona
description:
Barcelona's Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum is located inside a modernist palace, just minutes away from La Rambla.
The collection at the Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum in Barcelona is made up of rare objects related to all aspects of cannabis history and culture – prints, photos, artifacts, and more.
tagline: Learn about cannabis history and view ancient cultivation tools

title: Girona Day Trips from Barcelona
description: Go for a stroll through ancient streets or taste unique Catalan cuisine in beautiful cafes. Best of all, the convenient one-day format allows plenty of time to soak in the magic and charm of this historic region.
tagline: Soak in the sights of Girona on a day trip from Barcelona

title: Barcelona Photoshoots
description:
Capture your visit to the Catalonian capital with professional photoshoots!
Smile brightly and strike a pose against the backdrop of iconic monuments like the Sagrada Familia or Park Güell. Don't worry about awkwardly asking strangers to take a picture for you.
Enjoy your trip to Barcelona with perfect pictures to sum it up.
tagline: Complete your trip to Barcelona with stunning pictures clicked by a professional

title: Car Racing Simulator
description:
Committed to expanding gamification offerings by entering fully into the world of motorsport and eSports with a new sim racing experience in Barcelona, with the quality guarantee of DigaliX, partners in eSports, gamification, and sim racing.
Experience IMSIM Alma simulators, equipped to replicate the physics and sensations of real racing. Test your skills and feel the G-forces, grip limits, weight transfer, engine revs, ABS, the loss of traction, and more.
tagline: Grab the wheel, strap in, put on your headphones, and dive into the hyper-realistic world of racing

title: Hard Rock Cafe Barcelona
description: The Hard Rock Cafe Barcelona was established on November 10, 1997. Located on the famous Las Ramblas promenade and next to the historic Catalunya Square, the restaurant brings world-famous service to the heart of historic Barcelona. It blends perfectly with the local ambience of Catalunya with its inspired, creative cuisine and celebrated music memorabilia.

title: La Rambla
description: This is Barcelona's most famous street. 1.2 kilometres long, and surrounded with cultural centers, theatres and architecturally intriguing buildings, Las Ramblas is a tourist magnet and a perfect spot to dive into the Catalan culture. The street is set between narrow traffic lanes, flanked by plane trees and is crowded until late. A stroll on this street is pure amazement, with souvenirs hawkers, buskers, pavement artists, mimes and living statues all being a part of the ever-changing street scene.
tagline: Stroll down central Barcelona's most famous street

title: Estrella Damm Factory
description:
The Estrella Damm Factory is the manufacturer of a beer that's been in business since 1876! Visitors can tour the brewery and learn about the production process, as well as enjoy tastings.
The Estrella Damm Factory is located in El Prat de Llobregat near Barcelona and is easy to reach on public transport.

title: Tablao de Carmen
description: El Tablao de Carmen is a 30-year-old venue decorated in the style of an 'Andalusian corrala'. It showcases the best flamenco artists in front of an intimate audience in Barcelona's Poble Espanyol.

title: Espai CEL
description:
Espai CEL offers a series of seven thermal pools that encourage relaxation, restoration, and total bliss.
The ancient thermal tanks here have been in use since the 18th century. You can unwind with bubbles, water jets, hot benches, tea, and more.
Bliss awaits in each special pool in the historic center of the Roman village of Caldes de Montbui, Spain.

title: Torres Winery
description: Established in 1870 and now Spain's largest winery, this family-run business knows a thing or two about wine. Located in the Denomination of Origin (DO) of Penedès, near Barcelona, guests can visit the Mas La Plana vineyard, where one of the world's most prestigious red wines is produced. You can also enjoy a tasting of the onsite sommelier's selections and eat at the garden restaurant El Celleret.
tagline: Taste red wines and tour the cellars

title: Barcelona Sailing
description:
Experience the thrill of sailing in Barcelona! 
Watch the sun set over the Mediterranean Sea from the deck of a catamaran, and discover the city's unique skyline from a whole new perspective. 
Feel the wind in your hair and the ocean spray on your face as you sail along the coast, taking in the stunning views of the vibrant city. Enjoy the relaxed atmosphere and take the opportunity to soak up the city's fascinating cultural and historical atmosphere.
tagline: Enjoy an unforgettable experience, sailing along the coast of Barcelona

title: Barcelona Wine Tours
description:
Experience the best of Catalonian wine culture with an unforgettable wine tour from Barcelona! 
Take your pick from wine tours within the city, or venture out to Montserrat to visit some vineyards.With knowledgeable guides leading the way, you will get to taste some of the region's best wines, all while  learning about the region's unique and fascinating wine history. 
Discover the secrets behind the perfect wine pairing, and enjoy exclusive wines in stunning surroundings. 
Whether you are a connoisseur of fine wines or just want to learn more, these tours are sure to delight.
tagline: Explore Barcelona's wine culture on a delectable tour with plenty of tastings

title: Las Golondrinas de Barcelona
description: You've likely seen the Las Golondrinas boats docked in the harbor in Barcelona. These wooden double-decker boats have been built in the traditional way for generations and offer passenger rides along the Barcelona coastline.

title: Casino Barcelona
description:
Casino Barcelona is a world of entertainment open to everyone that combines gastronomy, live music, events, shows and gambling in an extraordinary atmosphere. Learn to play like a pro and have fun at Casino Barcelona. 
With its space of more than 7,000 square meters, you will find a wide variety of tables and machines in a spectacular game room, where you can enjoy traditional games (roulette and poker) or gambling machines, various restaurants, and concerts.
tagline: Learn to play like a pro and have fun at Casino Barcelona.

title: Ciutadella Park
description: Welcome to Ciutadella Park, a lush and enchanting green space located in the heart of Barcelona. Spread across 70 acres, Ciutadella Park is a haven of tranquility amidst the bustling city. As you enter the park, you'll be greeted by sprawling lawns, picturesque ponds, and majestic palm trees. Take a leisurely stroll along the winding pathways, and discover hidden gardens, charming fountains, and ornate sculptures that adorn the park. At the center of Ciutadella Park, you'll find the Cascada Monumental, a stunning waterfall designed by Josep Fontsére and Antoni Gaudí. The park offers a perfect setting for relaxation, picnics, and outdoor activities. It's a popular spot for locals and visitors to enjoy a peaceful moment, practice yoga, have a leisurely bike ride, or row a boat on the lake. Ciutadella Park also houses the Barcelona Zoo, where you can encounter a variety of animal species.
tagline: Nature's Oasis in the Heart of Barcelona: Ciutadella Park

title: Arc de Triomf
description: Welcome to the Arc de Triomf, a grand monument that stands as a symbol of Barcelona's triumphs and the city's vibrant spirit. Located at the top of Passeig de Lluís Companys, the Arc de Triomf serves as a majestic gateway to the city. As you approach this iconic landmark, you'll be captivated by its imposing structure and intricate architectural details. Built in a distinctive red brick style, the Arc de Triomf showcases ornate sculptures and reliefs that depict Barcelona's cultural and historical heritage. Pass under the arch, and you'll find yourself immersed in Parc de la Ciutadella, a beautiful park offering green spaces, serene lakes, and captivating sculptures. The Arc de Triomf serves as a meeting point for locals and visitors alike, who gather to enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and engage in leisurely activities such as picnics, cultural events, and performances. It's a place where the past and present converge, reflecting Barcelona's enduring spirit and celebration of triumph.
tagline: Gateway to Triumph and Celebration: Arc de Triomf, Barcelona

title: Rambla De Mar
description: Welcome to Rambla de Mar, a charming waterfront promenade located in Barcelona, Spain. Situated at the Port Vell, Rambla de Mar offers a delightful escape with stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea. As you stroll along this picturesque walkway, you'll be captivated by the serene ambiance and the gentle sea breeze. Enjoy the sight of sailboats and yachts docked in the marina, and admire the contemporary architecture of the surrounding buildings. Rambla de Mar is home to a variety of shops, restaurants, and cafes, offering an enticing array of local and international cuisines, as well as unique boutiques for shopping enthusiasts. Take a moment to relax at one of the seaside terraces, sip on a refreshing beverage, and soak in the beauty of the waterfront setting. Whether you're looking for a leisurely walk, a scenic dining experience, or simply a place to unwind by the sea, Rambla de Mar provides a tranquil haven where you can enjoy the best of Barcelona's coastal charm.
tagline: Coastal Tranquility and Mediterranean Delights: Rambla de Mar, Barcelona

title: Mercado de La Boqueria
description: With a rich history dating back to the 13th century, this market is not only a popular tourist attraction but also a beloved gathering place for locals. As you enter Mercado de La Boqueria, you'll be greeted by a sensory explosion of colors, aromas, and flavors. Explore the stalls filled with an abundance of fresh fruits, vegetables, seafood, meats, cheeses, and local delicacies. Immerse yourself in the lively atmosphere as vendors proudly showcase their products and offer samples of their specialties. Indulge in traditional Spanish tapas, sip on freshly squeezed juices, or satisfy your sweet tooth with an array of pastries and desserts. Mercado de La Boqueria is not just a market; it's a culinary adventure that allows you to experience the vibrant gastronomic culture of Barcelona. Join the locals and visitors alike as they come together to celebrate the richness of Catalan cuisine and the joy of communal gathering.
tagline: A Gastronomic Wonderland: Mercado de La Boqueria, Barcelona

title: Besalú Day Trips from Barcelona
description:
Experience a rush of excitement as you venture beyond Barcelona's city limits and into the heart and soul of the captivating Besalú. 
In this awe-inspiring medieval gem, each street and square unfolds a new chapter of history, cradling a distinctive charm that's sure to leave every traveler utterly enchanted. 
Pack a spirit of adventure for this delightful day trip, as Besalú’s astounding array of architectural marvels, uncompromised beauty, and rich cultural tapestry create a thrilling excursion that truly embodies the allure of Catalonia.
tagline: Venture beyond Barcelona to Besalú

title: America's Cup Experience
description: Try out a simulator to feel the excitement of sailing. Learn the rules of the oldest boat racing competition, meet the teams, and see how they race. Experience the thrill of the America's Cup firsthand.

Wine and Gogh is a fun and social painting experience, combining artistic creativity with a relaxed, friendly atmosphere. 
Paint under the guidance of an instructor while enjoying a glass of wine. The use of fluorescent paint adds a magical touch as it glows under ultraviolet lights. Learn basic painting techniques and watch your artwork come to life with vibrant colors and unique effects.

description: Gaudí Year celebrates the life and work of Antoni Gaudí through exhibitions, events, and cultural activities across Barcelona, marking a key anniversary of the architect’s legacy.
