title: Seine River Cruises
description:
When in Paris, a cruise on the Seine is a must. But how to choose from the many Seine river cruises on offer? Whether you're looking for a simple sightseeing cruise or something a bit more classy, there are plenty of choices available. 
If it's relaxation and education you want, some Seine cruises come with an included audio guide so that you can take in the city with an expert. Elsewhere there are aperitif, lunch, and gourmet dinner options, so you can cruise around the city to your heart's (and stomach's) content.
You can find discounts, deals, inclusions, and more info about Seine boat tours on this page. All that's left is to hop aboard and explore!
tagline: Admire Paris in the most beautiful way... on the Seine!

title: Palace of Versailles
description:
This luxurious 17th-century French palace is a masterclass in excess. It has hundreds of acres of magnificently landscaped gardens, more than 2,300 rooms, a large collection of French Renaissance art, an opera house, and a 75-meter-long hall of mirrors lined with priceless statues and busts. There are no half-measures in its conception or execution – it even has more than 1,200 fireplaces!
It's one of the most visited attractions in France, as well as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Take your pick of Palace of Versailles tickets and tours – you won't regret it!
tagline: Book your Palace of Versailles tickets in advance for timed entry, instant digital delivery, and smooth access to the Sun King’s estate

title: Musée d'Art Moderne de Paris
description: See roughly 15,000 works of modern and contemporary art spanning the 20th and 21st centuries. Attend special exhibitions and cultural events.
tagline: View contemporary art and attend special exhibitions

title: Musée d'Orsay
description:
Housed in the stunning Beaux-Arts style Gare d'Orsay railway station, the Musée d'Orsay is home to significant and stunning works by painters including Gauguin, Monet, Manet, Degas, Renoir, Cézanne, and Van Gogh.
It's widely considered to be one of the most culturally important museums in the world and hosts the most significant Impressionist and Post-Impressionist works ever created.
tagline: Behold breathtaking Impressionist masterpieces in the magnificent Orsay station in Paris

title: Disneyland® Paris
description:
When you’re this close to the magic, why not? Add one Disney day to your Paris stay!
Disney® Parks are full of activities for all ages, so get ready to escape to your favorite worlds from Disney, Pixar, Marvel, and Star Wars across two parks.
There are 50+ attractions, more than 70 restaurants, and all of your favorite characters ready to snap a selfie with you throughout the parks!
Older kids will find lots to enjoy – like Big Thunder Mountain and Star Wars Hyperspace Mountain, to name just a couple. With themed attractions, parades, unreal meals, and adventures, every aspect of your experience is filled with Disney magic. 
The only question is: how long do you want to stay? But one thing's for sure – two parks are better than one, so make the most of your visit to Disneyland Paris®.
tagline: Get instant access to the fairytale world of Disney with a ticket option that provides the freedom to create your perfect adventure across both parks

title: Fondation Louis Vuitton
description: Opened in October 2014, the Frank Gehry-designed Fondation Louis Vuitton is a stunning building dedicated to art, culture, and heritage. It can be found in the Jardin d'Acclimatation in Paris's 16th arrondissement.
tagline: Visit art exhibitions and view modern architecture

title: Sainte-Chapelle
description: Sainte-Chapelle is a Gothic-style royal chapel, known for its magnificent stained glass windows and for housing the Crown of Thorns. It is located on the Île de la Cité, in Paris.
tagline: See stunning stained glass light fill this 13th-century Parisian chapel

title: Musée de l'Armée & Napoleon's Tomb
description:
The Musée de l'Armée is part of a complex of buildings, home to museums and monuments, all relating to France's military history. A visit provides a fascinating look at prestigious old weapons, uniforms and armour. Napoleon Bonaparte's gilded tomb is also here, so be sure to pay your respects. 
The Musée de l'Armée is located on Rue de Grenelle, at Paris's Les Invalides. It's a great spot in Paris’ 7th arrondissement, near Champs-de-Mars and the French National Assembly.
tagline: Immerse yourself in history and discover an exceptional site

title: Musée Marmottan Monet
description:
Musée Marmottan Monet is the best place on Earth to see Claude Monet's masterpieces up close. Visitors can admire over 100 of the Frenchman's works, donated by friends and family over many years, including iconic pieces like 'Impression, Sunrise' and a unique set of his 'Water lilies'.
It's not just Monet that makes the museum such a must-see in Paris. Your Musée Marmottan Monet tickets allow you to enjoy fellow Impressionist icons like Gauguin, Degas, and Morisot, as well as a calendar of highlight exhibitions.
tagline: View Monet's paintings and browse the collection of Impressionist art

title: Arc de Triomphe
description:
The Arc de Triomphe is Napoleon's Neoclassical take on the ancient Roman triumphal arch. It's at the center of a square, with a dozen Parisian avenues radiating from it into the rest of the City of Light. 
At the base of the arch, the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier is rekindled at 18:30 (6:30 pm) each evening.
tagline: Get the best views of Paris and the Eiffel Tower from this Neoclassical landmark

title: Aquarium de Paris
description:
The Paris Aquarium is not only Europe's oldest aquarium (it was built in 1867), it was also Europe's largest for years. Housed in the center of the city – right next to the Eiffel Tower – on the quarries where Napoleon would house his cavalry, this institution has been part of the fabric of Parisian life for more than a century.
With 4 million liters of water, and 13,000 fish from around the world, there's plenty to keep you and your family entertained here for hours on end.
tagline: Watch sharks and touch sea stars

title: La Cinémathèque Française
description:
Located in a breathtaking postmodern building designed by Frank Gehry, the Cinémathèque Française is a French non-profit film organization that houses one of the largest archives of film documents and film-related objects in the world.
Alongside daily showings of global cinemas on their film screens, the museum is also home to the Méliès Museum which showcases the life of French cinematic pioneer George Méliès.

title: Musée Rodin Paris
description: The Musée Rodin in central Paris is dedicated to the life and work of French sculptor, Auguste Rodin. The French Impressionist sculptor crafted his later works in Hôtel Biron, which is now the Musée Rodin. Rodin donated much of his life's work to the French state, on condition that Hôtel Biron be converted into a museum celebrating his art.
tagline: Enter Rodin's palace of sculpture and see his famous Thinker

title: Musée national Picasso-Paris
description:
This museum is located in a stunning 17th century hôtel particulier in the Marais district of Paris. It's dedicated, as the name suggests, to the great artist Pablo Picasso. Descendants of the Spanish-born artist sowed the seeds for this gallery with works they offered to the French government in lieu of inheritance tax. French law allows for, in exceptional circumstances, the payment of inheritance taxes with artworks instead of money. This exception is only made if the art is an important contribution to France's cultural heritage - which is of course true in the case of Picasso.
With a permanent collection, temporary exhibitions, and an in-depth archive, this is one of the most important collections of works by one of the 20th century's greatest artists.
tagline: Welcome to Picasso’s world, the most famous artist of the 20th Century

title: Palais Galliera
description:
Palais Galliera is a Renaissance Revival building in Paris, France. Built in 1895, the grand palace has been home to Paris's fashion museum since 1977.
It now holds over 200,000 items including clothing, accessories, photos, and sketches, dating back as far as the 1700s. The museum hosts popular temporary exhibitions throughout the year.
tagline: View fashion exhibits and attend temporary art shows

title: Musée de l'Orangerie
description:
In 1922 Claude Monet offered eight of his giant Water Lilies panels to the French state, as a way of commemorating the end of WWI. His only condition was that they be displayed in a suitable venue. The French government found the perfect spot: the Orangerie in the Tuileries Garden. 
The Orangerie was built in 1852 as a winter shelter for the orange trees that lined the garden of the Tuileries Palace. For a period of time it was also used as a storehouse for goods, a bunk for soldiers, and as a flexible exhibition space. Monet's generous gift transformed it into the Musée de l'Orangerie, a pre-eminent gallery of Impressionist and post-Impressionist paintings.
The huge (and hugely impressive) Monet paintings are definitely the main event, but they are far from the only reason to visit. The museum also contains works by Cézanne, Matisse, Modigliani, Picasso, Renoir, and Rousseau, among others.
tagline: Admire Monet's Water Lilies and more Impressionist masterpieces inside

title: Palais Garnier
description:
This 19th-century architectural masterpiece was built by Charles Garnier at the wishes of Emperor Napoleon III. It's located at the Place de l'Opera, a square in Paris's 9th arrondissement.
The insides of the Palais Garnier, also known as the Opéra Garnier, boast some stunning Beaux-Arts architecture, like the 30-m high marble Grand Staircase. Its intricate interior inspired Gaston Leroux's 1910 novel The Phantom of the Opera (and thus Andrew Lloyd Webber's smash-hit musical), making this the perhaps the most famous opera house in the world.
tagline: Visit one of the world's most famous opera houses in Paris

title: Eiffel Tower
description:
When the Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889, it was the tallest building in the world. With 18,000 metallic parts and 2.5 million rivets, it was a true feat of engineering – and the perfect centerpiece for that year’s World’s Fair. But Alexandre Gustave Eiffel's tower had as many haters as it had fans.
However, over time, the Eiffel Tower has become a universally beloved icon; it's now the most-visited paid tourist attraction in the world. The fact remains that no trip to Paris is complete without a visit to the Eiffel Tower.
tagline: Tour Gustave Eiffel's world-famous tower and opt to visit the summit to get amazing views of Paris!

title: Paris Zoological Park (Parc Zoologique de Paris)
description:
Formerly known as the Bois de Vincennes Zoological Park, the 14.5-hectare Paris Zoological Park recently underwent two years of refurbishment works, giving this famous zoological park major natural environmental features, and plenty of room for the animals to roam. 
The animal habitats have been grouped five 'biozones', and the zoo has six restaurants and a 4,000 m2 tropical rainforest-filled greenhouse. It's visible from quite far away, thanks to the Big Rock, a 65m-tall artificial mini-mountain, which is an iconic symbol of the Paris Zoological Park.
tagline: Watch giraffes and feed lemurs

title: Musée Jacquemart-André
description: Private museum Jacquemart-André is located in Paris’s 8th arrondissement on the Boulevard Haussmann. It's easy to reach on metro stops Miromesnil, Monceau and Saint-Philipe-du-Roule and it's also within walking distance of the Champs-Élysées and Saint-Lazare train station.
tagline: View art collections and tour the historic mansion

title: Panthéon
description: The Panthéon is a beautiful, neo-Classical church located in the Latin Quarter of Paris, easy to reach on foot and by public transport. Originally dedicated to St. Genevieve (the patron saint of Paris), it's now a secular mausoleum that houses the remains of many French luminaries, Among those buried here are Voltaire, Rousseau, Victor Hugo, Marie Curie, and Émile Zola.
tagline: Visit historical crypts and view Foucault's pendulum

title: Paris Hop-on Hop-off Tours
description:
Explore the magical city of Paris with a convenient hop-on hop-off bus tour! 
See the historic City of Light in all its glory, from the riverside to the bustling streets. You'll get an up-close look at the famous monuments, cobblestone alleys, and chic boutiques. 
Take in the sights and sounds of Paris while learning interesting facts from onboard commentary.
tagline: Discover the enchantment of Paris on a hop-on hop-off tour

title: Bourse de Commerce - Pinault Collection
description:
Bourse de Commerce - Pinault Collection is a contemporary art haven, the fruit of a collaboration between renowned art collector Francois Pinault and Pritzker Prize-winning architect Tadao Ando.
This 30,000-square-foot museum, a renovation of Paris's domed, 18th-century corn exchange, is one of the city's newest major museums, costing almost $200 million to realize. Hosting 10 or more temporary exhibitions per year, visitors can expect to see a wealth of famous works by the world's greatest contemporary artists, as well as high-profile loans from notable institutions around the globe.
tagline: Visit modern art exhibits and attend cultural events

title: Atelier des Lumières
description: View impressive digital art projections. Attend special exhibits featuring famous artists.
tagline: Watch digital art shows and learn about famous artists

title: Musée du quai Branly - Jacques Chirac
description:
Suspended on a series of pillars, the Musée du quai Branly – Jacques Chirac features a vertical garden on the Seine side and enormous multicolored blocks on another. And once inside the grounds, the horizontal garden is full of birds and plants, walkways and paths. 
Inside the museum, it's an anthropological journey around the world via art and artifacts. The permanent collection reaches back as far as the Neolithic period, and spans Oceania, Asia, Africa and the America.
tagline: An anthropological journey around the world via art and artifacts

title: Louvre Museum
description:
The Louvre's magnificent classical silhouette (and funky glass pyramid) are well-known attractions in the center of Paris. But as nice as the Louvre is from the outside, its real magic is in the inside.
The Louvre's collection ranges from Mesopotamian, Egyptian, and Greek antiquities, with masterpieces by Da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Rembrandt. With 35,000 artifacts displayed at any given moment, it's a big, and worthwhile, commitment. 
The Louvre, known as the world's largest museum, is a cultural icon in Paris. A pre-booked, timed-entry ticket is now required for entry. This ensures you can explore its vast collection without long waits.
tagline: Secure your Louvre Museum tickets online to fully explore Paris' monumental art collection and cultural artifacts from around the globe

title: Grévin Wax Museum
description:
At Grévin Wax Museum you’ll meet world-famous celebrities and fascinating figures. George Clooney and Franz Kafka, or Angelina Jolie, and the alchemy-loving emperor, Rudolf II and his many potions.
The museum also has some surprisingly hands-on exhibits. Kids will love the Discovery Tour where they can see how a wax figure is created. Touch wax and resin and see the fake ears and eyes that help bring a statue to life.
tagline: Pose with wax figures and walk through themed rooms

title: Petit Palais
description:
The Petit Palais/Musée des Beaux-Arts de la Ville de Paris is a beautiful art museum in Paris located close to the Champs-Élysées – Clemenceau metro station.
View a diverse collection of fine arts, including paintings and sculptures. Attend temporary exhibitions that cover a wide range of artistic movements and styles.
tagline: View art collections and visit the decorative arts section

title: Musée Banksy Paris
description:
Musée Banksy is dedicated to the street-art movement and its elusive and enigmatic poster boy: Banksy. The exhibition features recreations of the artist's most iconic works by other street artists, and implores the viewer to think about the purpose and essence of what art is. 
If you are a fan of the cuttingly satirical art of Banksy, this is an absolute must.
tagline: Learn about street art and its global impact

title: Grande Galerie de l'Évolution
description: The Grande Galerie de l'Évolution is home to over 7,000 preserved animal specimens, all taking residence in a huge 19th-century hall in the light of a massive glass roof. It's an evolutionary adventure located in Paris, France.
tagline: Explore life-sized animal displays and interactive exhibits

title: Château de Fontainebleau
description:
Château de Fontainebleau is just 55 kilometres from the center of Paris. This massive palace has over 1500 rooms sprawled out on 130 acres of gardens and parks. It's one of the largest royal French châteaux to have ever been built. 
Fontainebleau was much loved by Napoléon Bonaparte, who oversaw a lot of the restoration work. Napoléon III was another regular visitor, but as an example of the changes it has seen, during WWII the château was a headquarter for occupying Germans. It's now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: Aquaboulevard
description: The largest water park in Paris and one of the premier water parks in Europe, Aquaboulevard is the perfect family-friendly day out in Paris. The park features a wide array of waterslides, wave pools and other thrilling aquatic amenities, as well as more relaxed areas with huge jacuzzis and relaxation areas.

title: Parc Astérix
description: Parc Astérix is an action-packed theme park based on the stories of the Asterix comics. Located just outside of Paris, this theme park is famous for its high-octane roller coasters and adrenaline-filled activities for thrill-seekers of all ages.
tagline: It's all about the Gaul at this action-packed theme park near Paris

title: Château de Chantilly
description: Château de Chantilly is a princely domain just a few kilometers outside of Paris, which provides a great look into the life and loves of French VIPs. The castle houses the Condé Museum and its princely apartments, an impressive collection of paintings, a library, and archives holding over 13,000 books, including the most valuable manuscript in the world.
tagline: Horses, castles, forest walks and picnic spots – the perfect day out from Paris

title: Ballon de Paris Generali
description:
The Ballon de Paris Generali has been around since 1999 in the André Citroën Park (15th arrondissement), and flown over 500,000 passengers. 
The world's largest balloon also functions as an air quality indicator, so you can really find out what the air's like up there.

title: Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes
description: Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes is home to 1,200 animals, right in the center of Paris. It's one of the world's oldest zoos and also runs a breeding programme for endangered species. Visitors can mingle with mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians and other animals known as humans.
tagline: See red pandas and visit the historic zoo buildings

title: Musée National de la Marine
description:
At the Palais de Chaillot overlooking the Trocadéro Gardens, the Musée National de la Marine has reopened after six years of renovation. 
Learn France's maritime and naval history through a variety of interactive exhibits. From the days of wooden sailing ships to the age of modern cruise ships, you'll leave the museum with newfound knowledge about French maritime history.
Get ready to take a step back in time, taking a journey from Paris to Le Havre, braving storms and shipwrecks, and learn about 250 years of French maritime and naval history.
tagline: View historic ship models and learn about naval history

title: Musée National des Arts Asiatiques Guimet
description: View a large collection of Asian art and artifacts. Attend educational programs on Asian cultures and history.
tagline: See ancient artifacts and visit Buddhist art collections

title: Jardin d'Acclimatation
description:
Jardin d'Acclimatation began as a French take on the English Garden way back in 1860. Once a temporary home to the animals brought back to France from the colonies, the modern Jardin d'Acclimatation retains much of its classic charm.
With over 40 family-fun attractions and over 400 animals on site, a day at Jardin d'Acclimatation has something for everyone. Enjoy rollercoasters, rides, and activities galore! Follow the squawks and chirps inside the Great Aviary, and stroll the park's grand 45-acre gardens.
tagline: Experience a world of adventure just half an hour from central Paris!

title: Catacombs of Paris
description:
Easily one of the creepiest places on Earth, Les Catacombes de Paris (Catacombs of Paris) contains the remains of six million Parisians – that's almost three times the city's current population!
This macabre monument is in central Paris, and sometimes, right under your feet.
tagline: Get subterranean in this macabre tour of what lies beneath the City of Lights

title: Fondation Cartier pour l'Art Contemporain
description:
The Fondation Cartier pour l'Art Contemporain, based in Paris since 1994, resides in a transparent building designed by architect Jean Nouvel. 
Each year, the Fondation Cartier organizes thematic or solo exhibitions, commissioning artists to add to its extensive collection.
tagline: Explore the rotating exhibitions at Fondation Cartier pour l'Art Contemporain

title: Carnavalet Museum
description: After four years of major renovation work, the Carnavalet Museum reopened its doors in May 2021. The oldest museum in Paris has had a real rebirth, so expect a breath of new life in a museum that's nearly 150 years old! You'll find more than 625,000 works that tell the story of Paris's past inside.
tagline: View historical artifacts and learn about the French Revolution

title: Cité des sciences et de l'industrie
description: Paris's Cité des sciences et de l'industrie is the biggest science museum in Europe. Surrounded by a big green park, this science and tech center has a number of scientific attractions. Highlights include an IMAX theater, a planetarium, and the decommissioned submarine Argonaute.

title: Musée de l’Homme
description: The Musée de l'Homme is an anthropology museum located in Paris, France. It was first established in 1937 by Paul Rivet as part of the 1937 Exposition Internationale des Arts et Techniques dans la Vie Moderne.
tagline: Visit ancient artifacts and learn about human evolution

title: Musée des Arts et Métiers
description:
Musée des Arts et Métiers is a museum of industrial design in Paris, France, founded upon the principle of preserving groundbreaking artifacts of scientific importance.
Notable items in its vast collection include an original version of the Foucault pendulum, the original model of the Statue of Liberty, some of the first planes, and Blaise Pascal's Pascaline (the first mechanical calculator).
tagline: View historic inventions and visit the Foucault pendulum

title: Bibliothèque nationale de France François-Mitterrand
description: Explore the extensive collection of books and manuscripts. Attend various exhibitions and cultural events held throughout the year.
tagline: View rare manuscripts and visit the modern reading rooms

title: Paris Cabaret Shows
description:
Experience the soul of Paris by booking a cabaret show and bask in the timeless spectacle of music, dance and comedy! Enjoy the allure of the city and the sparkle of the stage, as you watch a breathtaking display of top cabaret talent. 
Let a night of Parisian cabaret take you on a journey of enchantment, as you laugh and gasp at the glitzy, glamorous spectacle that has been entertaining the public in Paris for over a century. So don't wait, book your cabaret show today and let the show begin!
tagline: Let the magic of Paris begin and book a cabaret show today!

title: Hôtel de la Marine
description:
Hôtel de la Marine is a cultural icon in the heart of Paris. With a history stretching back to 1757, this grand monument on Place de la Concorde was home to Royal France's ever-moving collection of treasures and priceless furniture, before serving as the navy headquarters for almost 200 years. 
Only opened to the public in 2021 after a major refurbishment, the Hôtel de la Marine is a lesser-known cultural gem in Paris, just a 15-minute walk from the Louvre and Musée d'Orsay.
tagline: Explore the 18th-century apartments of the Hôtel de la Marine

title: Musée de la Libération de Paris - Musée du Général Leclerc - Musée Jean Moulin
description: The Musée de la Libération de Paris was inaugurated in 2019 to celebrate 75 years since the city was liberated from Nazi occupation. Hear the fascinating story of how proud, ordinary Parisians stood up to German rule and retook their city over six days in 1944.

title: Notre-Dame Cathedral of Paris
description: Observe the detailed Gothic architecture and stained glass windows. Climb the towers for a view of the city.
tagline: Visit the bell towers and view the gargoyles

title: ECLIPSO Paris
description:
Put on your VR headset with Eclipso to see historical scenes up close through engaging storytelling. 
You'll feel like you're traveling to different times and places, combining culture with new technology.
tagline: Use technology to explore a different time and place

title: Cité Immersive des Fables
description: Explore interactive exhibits and learn about different cultures. Attend workshops and participate in hands-on activities.
tagline: Visit the immersive rooms and 360º projections

title: Le Grand Rex
description: Le Grand Rex is a cinema and concert venue in Paris, France. A shining example of the Art Deco movement, Le Grand Rex is noted for its opulent decor and its enormous main auditorium. It also happens to be the largest cinema theater in Europe.

title: Musée Cognacq-Jay
description: Musée Cognacq-Jay is a museum packed with 18th-century art and wonders in a classic French Renaissance mansion, located in the Marais District in Paris. The museum's collection was brought together by Ernest Cognacq and his wife Marie-Louise Jay between 1900 and 1925. This VIP couple had quite an influence on Paris, as they also founded the department store La Samaritaine, though they invested the majority of their fortune in French 18th-century works of art.

title: Paradox Museum Paris
description: Paradox Museum opens its doors in Paris, in a unique, giant space of 1700 m2 in the heart of the capital, a stone's throw from the Opera. The whole family will be immersed in Paradox Museum's array of visual, sensory and educational experiences. With more than 90 astonishing exhibitions inspired by paradoxes, the Paradox Museum Paris experiments with your senses and combines the world of science, art and human perception.

title: Château de Vaux-le-Vicomte
description: The Château de Vaux-le-Vicomte is a Baroque French château located 55 kilometers southeast of Paris. The château's ornate architecture and impressive manicured gardens are said to have been the inspiration for the construction of the more famous Palace of Versailles.
tagline: Tour the grand halls and walk through the expansive gardens

title: Conciergerie
description: The Conciergerie is located on the Île de la Cité, in Paris. This marvelous Gothic building preceded the Palais du Louvre as the royal seat of power in France but was later used as a prison during the French Revolution's Reign of Terror.

title: Musée de la Vie Romantique
description: The Musée de la Vie Romantique is the place for falling in love... with contemporary art! It is located at the foot of Montmartre, in the 9th arrondissement of Paris, France. Spend some time starry-eyed in this 1830s hôtel particulier, which has studios, a greenhouse, a small garden, and a paved courtyard. Permanent collections are free.

title: Paris Airport Transfers
description:
Make the most of your time in Paris by choosing a convenient and stress-free airport transfer option! Skip the long taxi lines, and enjoy a comfortable and reliable transfer from the airport to your destination. 
These airport transfers will ensure a hassle-free start to your stay in the City of Light. Once you've arrived, you'll be ready to explore the city and take in the sights. Book now and start your journey in style!
tagline: Skip the taxi lines and start your Paris journey in style!

title: Maison Victor Hugo
description:
Standing alongside the likes of Shakespeare and Company, Café de Flore, and Oscar Wilde's grave at Père Lachaise Cemetery, Maison Victor Hugo is one of the most treasured literary pilgrimage sites in Paris. 
The luminary Romantic writer responsible for timeless masterpieces like Les Miserables, The Hunchback of Notre-Dame, and countless other works of genius, Victor Hugo spent 16 years of his incredible (and prolific) life in this sublime Parisian townhouse. 
These days the building is one of the 14 City of Paris Museums and is one of the best museums in Paris. And that's saying something!
tagline: Tour the historic rooms and view original manuscripts

title: Musée de Montmartre
description: Immerse yourself in the artistic heritage of Paris' bohemian past, where artists like Renoir, Valadon, and Utrillo once lived and created their masterpieces. The museum's location, complete with gardens and views of the city, offers a retreat from the bustling streets of the Montmartre district.
tagline: See where Renoir, Valadon and Utrillo lived and worked

title: Safari de Thoiry
description:
Escape from the concrete jungle and return to nature at Safari de Thoiry, located just 35 minutes from Paris by car.
Hop in your car and drive an 8 km trail around the park and get up close to 800 different animals! You can even hop out of your vehicle and walk through a glass tunnel, which lets you see the animals up close – you might end up with a couple of lions lying on top of you!

title: Dalí Paris
description: Dalí Paris is home to the biggest collection of Salvador Dalí's work in France, with over 300 pieces across its permanent collection. It is located in Montmartre, Paris, France.

title: SEA LIFE Paris Val d'Europe
description: SEA LIFE Paris Val d'Europe is a family-friendly exhibition space that's home to hundreds of sea creatures in an immersive setting. Kids will love touching starfish and rays in the touch pools, and watching the king penguins strutting their stuff. It is located in Serris, France.

title: Château de Vincennes
description:
At various times in its history, the Château de Vincennes has been a royal residence, a porcelain factory, a prison and a burial site. Its location was designed to allow the monarchy to keep a watchful eye over Paris. 
Now, with a metro stop named after it, it's a part of the French capital. Highlights include the highest fortified tower in Europe and the beautiful Gothic Sainte-Chapelle de Vincennes.

title: The Archeological Crypt of the Île de la Cité
description: The archaeological crypt of the Island City is a museum of the city of Paris, just beneath the parvis of Notre Dame Cathedral. It presents archaeological vestiges of antiquity, to the 19th century, discovered during excavations between 1965 and 1972.
tagline: Learn about ancient ruins and historical artifacts

title: Notre Dame Space – Eternal Notre-Dame
description:
The Eternal Notre-Dame exhibition and experience is dedicated to bringing to life the past of the iconic landmark, the Notre Dame of Paris. 
With the aim of reviving the Notre Dame following the 2019 fire, this virtual reality show equips its visitors with immersive gear to time-travel as far back as the 13th century up to the 21st century on a tour of the monument's historical changes.
tagline: Experience Notre-Dame's evolution and restoration journey with immersive VR

title: Bruges Day Trips from Paris
description:
Take a break from the hustle and bustle of Paris with a day trip to Bruges! Explore the picturesque canals, cobblestone streets and charming buildings of this beautiful city in northern Belgium. 
Discover all of its cultural attractions and historical landmarks, and don't forget to treat yourself to some of the delicious local cuisine. With a day trip to Bruges, you can rest assured that you'll have a memorable experience.
tagline: Escape to Bruges and experience the medieval charm of northern Belgium!

title: Versailles Day Trips from Paris
description:
Venture into the regal splendor of a Versailles day trip from Paris, the perfect escape teeming with awe-inspiring grandeur! 
Lose yourself amid lavish gardens, extravagant rooms, and opulent halls, drenched in a rich swirl of history and luxury that leaves every visitor in awe. 
With each step, the bygone era's stories unweave, creating an unforgettable journey that you'll fondly recount for years to come.
tagline: Experience the royal treatment with a day trip to Versailles

title: Jeu de Paume
description: Nestled in the heart of the Tuileries Garden lies the historic Jeu de Paume, an iconic cultural institution and artistic hub, dedicated to showcasing images from the 20th and 21st centuries. This includes photography, video, contemporary art, cinema, and online creations. Through its curated exhibitions, film cycles, seminars, educational activities, and publications, Jeu de Paume seeks not only to highlight the work of renowned artists such as Richard Avedon, Diane Arbus, and Sally Mann, but also to introduce emerging talents like Omer Fast and Ali Cherri. Each exhibition tells a unique story, whether historical or contemporary, and attracts an increasingly diverse audience. Jeu de Paume is more than an art center

title: Geology and Mineralogy Gallery
description: The Mineralogy and Geology Gallery is a part of the National Museum of Natural History. It is situated in the Jardin des Plantes. The gallery displays a great collection of crystals, gemstones and minerals.

title: Mont Saint-Michel Day Trips from Paris
description: Embark on a magical day trip from Paris to the stunning Mont Saint-Michel. Marvel at the architectural wonder, enjoy panoramic views, and discover the rich history of this iconic landmark.
tagline: Step into a fairytale on our Mont Saint-Michel day trips from Paris

title: Musée du Chocolat
description: Musée du Chocolat is a museum focused on the history of chocolate, from cacao harvesting to the final product. It's a lesson in indulgence.
tagline: Everything you never knew about chocolate, all in one place

title: Château de Pierrefonds
description: Château de Pierrefonds is a magnificent castle designed and rebuilt in a medieval style by Napoleon III and his architect Eugène Viollet-le-Duc. It is located Pierrefonds, France, not too far by car from Paris.

title: Paris Self-Guided Tours
description: Explore Paris at your own pace with the self-guided tours. Choose from a range of options, including walking tours, bike tours, and museum tours, and discover the city's rich history, art, and culture.
tagline: Discover Paris your way with our self-guided tours

title: Basilique Cathédrale de Saint-Denis
description: The Basilique Cathédrale de Saint-Denis is the world's first Gothic cathedral, completed in the 13th century. It's the final resting place for French royalty and the burial place of 43 kings, 32 queens and 10 servants to the monarchy.

title: Cité de l'Économie
description: Explore interactive exhibits about economic concepts. Attend workshops and presentations on financial literacy.
tagline: Learn about money and participate in interactive exhibits

title: Le Palais de Tokyo
description: Paris's rule-breaking museum showcases only the most cutting-edge of modern art, with regular programs of exhibitions sure to delight, inspire and provoke in equal measure.

title: Roland Garros Stadium
description: The Roland-Garros Stadium is the home of the French Open. The 34-acre complex contains twenty courts and is arguably the most famous clay court tournament in the world of competitive tennis.

title: Paris Walking Tours
description:
Explore the beauty and culture of Paris on an engaging walking tour! Take a stroll through the City of Light and explore the iconic sights and hidden gems of this remarkable city. Discover centuries-old architecture, taste the delicious local delicacies and get immersed in the city's vibrant culture. 
These walking tours are the perfect way to get to know the city and see the sights up close. With knowledgeable tour guides, you'll learn all about the history and culture of Paris. So why wait? Book your Paris walking tour now and create an unforgettable experience!
tagline: Unlock the magic of Paris and join a walking tour

title: Bibliothèque nationale de France Richelieu
description: Explore the extensive collections of books, manuscripts, and historical documents. Attend exhibitions showcasing art, history, and culture.
tagline: Visit the historic reading rooms and view ancient manuscripts

title: Paris City Cards
description:
Explore the City of Lights with the Paris City Card, the ultimate ticket to discovering the beauty of Paris. Take advantage of great savings on some of the best things to do in Paris, from iconic attractions to unique local experiences. 
See world-famous masterpieces, explore hidden gardens, and take a culinary journey through the city's best restaurants. With so much to explore and experience, the Paris City Card is the perfect way to make the most of your time in Paris.
tagline: Discover the City of Light with the Paris city card!

title: Père Lachaise Cemetery
description: With more than 3.5 million visitors every year, Père Lachaise Cemetery is the most visited graveyard in the world. The list of notable historical figures laid to rest here is expansive and features icons like Oscar Wilde, Jim Morrison, Edith Piaf, Marcel Proust, Gertrude Stein, Chopin, Pierre Bourdieu, and George-Eugène Haussmann – just to name a few. It's located in the 20th arrondissement of Paris.
tagline: Get spirited around the most-visited cemetery in the world

title: Perfume Museum
description:
Set in the heart of Paris, just a stone’s throw from the Opéra Garnier, the Musée du Parfum is a one-of-a-kind, fun and instructive museum concept, that brings perfume to life. 
Inside, you'll follow the manufacturing process from raw materials to harvesting, extraction, distillation, formulation, industrialization and bottling. There's also the chance to find out more about the creative process and the master noses required to create scents. The second section of the museum features an exceptional collection of ancient bottles retracing the history of fragrance from Ancient Egypt to the 20th century, including kohl pots, pomanders, vinaigrettes, perfume burners, pots-pourris, travel sets, smelling salt bottles and precious flasks.

title: Monnaie de Paris
description: Monnaie de Paris, or the Paris Mint, is a government-owned institution responsible for producing France's euro coins. Founded in 864 AD, it is the world's oldest continuously-running minting institution.
tagline: Tour the coin exhibits and visit the metalworking workshops

title: Cité de l'architecture et du patrimoine
description: Cité de l'architecture et du patrimoine (Architecture and Heritage City) is a museum for all lovers of architecture, whether they're new to the subject or if it's their passion and profession. Casts and miniatures of major French monuments from the 12th century to the present day are available for guests to admire, as well as a number of international buildings of great renown from the 19th century onwards.
tagline: View architectural models and visit the museum's galleries

title: Paris Bike Tours
description:
Paris is one of the most beautiful cities in the world, and a bike tour is the perfect way to explore it! Experience the sights, sounds, and smells of Paris from a unique and exciting perspective. 
These bike tours of Paris give you the opportunity to discover the city's hidden gems and iconic landmarks, all at your own pace. 
With a knowledgeable local guide, you'll have the chance to explore Paris like never before.
tagline: Discover Paris like never before on a two-wheeled adventure

title: Parrot World - The Immersive Animal Park
description: Experience the wonders of South America at Parrot World - the first immersive animal park in Île de France. Get up close to animals from the Amazon and Patagonia, including parrots, penguins, jaguars, and giant otters. Kids can enjoy a giant playground while you take a break in dedicated dining and picnic areas. And don't miss the immersive ranch, where you can meet alpacas, wallabies, and more. Come and discover the magic of South America at Parrot World!
tagline: Experience the wild wonders of South America just outside Paris

title: Giverny Day trips from Paris
description:
Take a day trip from Paris to the famous French impressionist village of Giverny. Enjoy a peaceful journey through the picturesque French countryside, and explore the charming village with its colorful gardens and stunning architecture. 
Get a unique perspective of the local sights and soak up the atmosphere of this beautiful destination. And don't forget to take lots of photos to capture the beauty of this place!
tagline: Capture the beauty of Giverny on a day trip from Paris!

title: Paris Food Tours
description: Experience the culinary delights of the City of Lights with a Paris food tour. Indulge your taste buds in a gastronomic adventure through the vibrant streets of Paris. An expert guides will lead you on a journey to savor the finest French cuisine, from mouthwatering pastries to exquisite cheeses and delectable wines.
tagline: Savor the flavors of Paris with a food tour around the city

title: Villa Savoye
description: Villa Savoye is the masterpiece of Swiss architect Charles-Édouard Jeanneret, aka Le Corbusier. Commonly cited as the basis for modern architecture, Villa Savoye, found in Poissy, France, is a classic example of Jeanneret's five points of architecture. The villa, less than an hour's journey from Paris, was built in 1929 and is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

title: Stade de France
description: The Stade de France is the national stadium of France, located in the commune of Saint-Denis, just north of Paris. The stadium is used by the national football team and rugby union team for international competitions. Its seating capacity of 80,698 makes it the 8th-largest stadium in Europe.

title: France Miniature
description: France Miniature lets visitors uncover the diverse regions of France by walking around the sites as a giant might! See a miniature Eiffel Tower, the Château Vaux le Vicomte, Saint-Tropez, Mont Saint-Michel, the Arles Amphitheatre, the Rosary Basilica at Lourdes, and other French landmarks in one park. There are also rides, slides and play areas for kids, plus two family restaurants on-site. France Miniature is located in Élancourt, France.

title: Cité Internationale de la Langue Française – Château de Villers-Cotterêts
description:
Nestled within the renovated walls of the Renaissance-era Villers-Cotterêts Castle, built by François I in 1532, the Cité Internationale de la Langue Française is a vibrant, living tribute to the French language. 
Dedicated to sharing and fostering a love for this ever-evolving language, this venue stretches over 1200m2 and offers your a rich cultural experience! You'll visit temporary exhibition halls, a performance room, courtyards, gardens, a café, and a bookstore. Consider this your open invitation to immerse yourself in French culture, right in the heart of the unique green haven that is the Retz Forest.

title: Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris
description: Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris is located in the iconic Hôtel Particulier, where Yves Saint Laurent spent almost 30 years designing his high fashion collections from 1974 to 2002.

title: Normandy Day Trips from Paris
description: Discover the historic and picturesque region of Normandy on a day trip from Paris. Visit the D-Day landing beaches, explore charming towns, and indulge in local cuisine.
tagline: Escape to Normandy for a day trip from Paris

title: Musée Zadkine
description: Musée Zadkine is a museum dedicated to the works of sculptor, Ossip Zadkine and his wife, artist Valentine Prax. It is located in the 6th arrondissement of Paris, not far from Jardin du Luxembourg.

title: Disneyland Paris Day Trips from Paris
description:
Get ready to step into a world of magic and enchantment, just a stone's throw away from Paris! 
With a Disneyland Paris day trip, the realm of beloved characters and fantastic adventures is closer than you might think. 
While this whimsical escape offers boundless fun for kids, grown-ups can relive their childhood memories, too.
tagline: Magic, enchantment and adventure at Disneyland Paris

title: Château de Champs-sur-Marne
description: The Château de Champs-sur-Marne is an 18th-century château roughly 25 kilometers east of Paris. The château boasts an 85-hectare garden and rococo style decor. It can be accessed by public transport with a journey time of roughly 40 minutes from Paris.

title: AccroCamp Créteil
description: Climb various rope courses and zip lines. Challenge different obstacle courses designed for all skill levels.
tagline: Climb through treetop courses and ride zip lines

title: Paradis Latin
description:
The Paradis Latin, in Paris's Latin Quarter, was built in 1803 on the orders of none other than Napoleon Bonaparte. It burned down in 1870 but was rebuilt in 1887-1889 by Mr. Eiffel Tower himself, Gustave Eiffel. After being closed for a great chunk of the 20th century it reopened in the 70s. It's been playing spectacular shows to packed houses ever since.
Shows here include a mix of trapeze artists, comedy, dancers, drag shows, magic, and of course, the famous cancan. Show tickets feature options for Champagne accompaniment or different meal options, to ensure that your trip to paradise leaves you satisfied.
tagline: See live shows, including a mix of trapeze artists, comedy, dancers, magic, and more

title: Virtual Room Paris
description: Solve puzzles in a team-based virtual reality experience. Engage in immersive missions and collaborate with friends.
tagline: Take on puzzle challenges using VR

title: Paris Workshops and Classes
description:
Discover the beauty and culture of Paris with one of these wonderful workshops and classes! From creative arts to cultural excursions, there is something for everyone in the City of Lights. 
Take in the local atmosphere, learn new skills, and make memories that will last a lifetime. Immerse yourself in the unique culture of Paris and explore the city like a local. Don’t wait - book your experience today!
tagline: Unlock the magic of Paris with one of these awesome cultural workshops

title: Ateliers-musée Chana Orloff
description: View sculptures and art pieces by Chana Orloff. Tour the artist's studio and learn about her creative process.
tagline: View Chana Orloff's sculptures and walk through her studio

title: Loire Valley Day Trips from Paris
description: Experience the beauty of the Loire Valley on a day trip from Paris! Enjoy a leisurely drive through the countryside, taking in the rolling hills and lush vineyards. Visit charming villages and discover the region's remarkable history and culture. Enjoy a delicious lunch and sample some of the region's renowned wines. Stroll through magnificent chateaux and gardens, and take stunning photos of the scenery.
tagline: Unforgettable sights and tastes await in the Loire Valley!

title: Château de Rambouillet
description: Château de Rambouillet is a beautiful home and former retreat for presidents, kings and VIPs in the town of Rambouillet, in the Île-de-France region of northern France. A visit here is a step back in time to see how some of the greatest men (and women) in history lived.

title: Champagne Day Trips from Paris
description:
Experience the classically sophisticated allure of champagne from the unparalleled comfort of a day excursion departing from beautiful Paris. 
Savor the distinct notes of France's renowned bubbly in the very vineyards where its cherished grapes are meticulously grown. 
Between sips, marvel at the enchanting charm of the rustic countryside, ensuring this trip becomes a memory to fondly celebrate.
tagline: Savor France's finest champagne and countryside charm

title: Paris Escape Room
description: Step into the heart of Gotham City at Batman Escape by Dama Dreams, nestled in the vibrant core of Paris. This is more than just a venue - it's an immersive bubble of playfulness and experience, inviting you to lose yourself in the world of Batman. Indulge in an expertly mixed cocktail, browse through a treasure trove of DC Comics merchandise, and plunge into three unique 'game-theatre' experiences that beautifully blur the lines between live-action role-play and immersive theatre. At Batman Escape, we redefine the traditional escape game, offering you an adventure enriched with interactive narratives and live actors. This isn't just a meeting place - it's a shared journey into the extraordinary.

title: Paris Comedy Shows
description:
Looking for a night to remember? Look no further! Paris is the home of some of the world's best comedy shows, and you don't have to look far to experience the laughter. Whether you're looking for a night out with the family or just a solo laugh, there's a perfect show for you. 
All you have to do is pick a date and get ready for a night of entertainment. Don't miss out on the chance to get tickets to one of Paris' legendary comedy shows!
tagline: Experience legendary laughter with the best Paris comedy shows

title: Paris Photoshoots
description:
Make your dreams come true with a photoshoot in Paris! Capture the romance of the City of Light with a professional photoshoot. Stroll through the cobblestone streets, explore the lush parks and gardens and take in the iconic architecture - all while a professional photographer captures your special moments. 
Get the perfect souvenir from your trip to Paris and create memories that will last a lifetime. Book your photoshoot today and see Paris like never before!
tagline: Capture the romance of Paris and book your photoshoot today!

title: Paris Luggage Storage
description:
Using luggage storage in Paris can make your trip much more convenient. It allows you to explore the city without the hassle of dragging your bags around. 
You can also enjoy those last few hours before your flight without worrying about where to keep your luggage.
tagline: Avoid dragging your bags around on your next trip to Paris

title: Maison de Balzac
description: Maison de Balzac is a museum located in the last-surviving residence of novelist Honoré de Balzac. It is here that Balzac edited his magnum opus, La Comédie Humaine, and wrote some of his finest novels, including La Rabouilleuse, Une ténébreuse affaire, and La Cousine Bette.

title: Musée Cernuschi
description:
Italian-French art collector Henri Cernuschi left his entire collection of Asian art to the people of Paris upon his death in 1896, and with it, an entire mansion to house his priceless wonders.
Musée Cernuschi is a treasure trove of Asian art. See prehistoric relics and ancient masterpieces from China, Japan, Korea, Vietnam, and elsewhere. The museum's permanent collection is free to access, but these Musée Cernuschi tickets grant you access to exclusive temporary exhibitions borrowed from institutions around the world.

title: Paris Wine & Cheese Tasting
description:
Discover the perfect combination of French delights! Enjoy an unforgettable experience in Paris with a wine and cheese tasting. Sample the finest cheeses and wines that the city has to offer and explore the diverse tastes of France. 
Take a guided tour of the city and learn more about the history and culture of Paris. Discover the secrets of some of the best wines and cheeses in the world and enjoy an essential Parisian experience.
tagline: Unlock the secrets of French culture with a wine and cheese tasting in Paris

title: Musée de la Poste
description:
Located in the heart of the Montparnasse district, facing the train station, the Musée de La Poste is a unique, unusual, contemporary museum designed to help you discover a world of postal history! Stroll around its historical (37,000 pieces), philatelic (400,000 pieces) and artistic (6,000 pieces of modern and contemporary art and experience the diversity of the postal world, from the first messengers to how things operate in the present day. 
You can also enjoy the temporary exhibitions held regularly on the ground floor of the museum.

title: Sherwood Parc
description:
Sherwood Parc is a leisure park offering a Robin Hood-themed slant on adventures in the forest. Located in the heart of the Parc Naturel Régional Oise - Pays de France in Val-d'Oise, it's easily accessible from Paris.
Kids and adults alike can make their own band of merry men and experience many different activities, from rope bridges to paintball and interactive workshops in the trees. Kids will love the Fun Box, a net play-space across 7 floors!

title: Les Caves du Louvre
description: Les Caves du Louvre is an 18th-century wine cellar that was once used by the King of France. Now, it's a pleasurable multisensory experience dedicated to informing visitors about the art (and science) of winemaking and wine-tasting. Distinguish a Chardonnay from a Chablis, a Beaujolais from a Burgundy, and everything in-between!

title: Expiatory Chapel
description: The Expiatory Chapel is a chapel dedicated to Marie Antoinette and Louis XVI, built in 1816 and inaugurated in 1826 by King Louis XVIII. The chapel is located on the original burial ground of the royals, though they now rest in the Basilica of St. Denis. The chapel is located in the eighth arrondissement of Paris, France, and the closest metro station is Saint-Augustin.

title: AccroCamp: Paris-Jablines
description: AccroCamp: Paris-Jablines is a family-friendly adventure park featuring ziplines, climbing walls and more in the treetops. There are different courses to cater to different experience levels, so everyone can test their physical prowess like Tarzan in a French forest. The Jablines campsite is situated 9 km from Disneyland Paris, France.

title: Paris City Tours
description:
Visit famous landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre Museum. 
Take a boat ride along the Seine River and see the city's historic architecture.
tagline: Visit the Eiffel Tower and cruise the Seine River

title: Église de la Madeleine
description: Standing proudly at the end of Rue Royale, Église de la Madeleine is a masterpiece of architectural grandeur and artistic beauty. As you approach the church, you'll be captivated by its imposing façade, adorned with majestic columns and intricate sculptures. Step inside and be awed by the soaring ceilings, elegant marble columns, and ornate decorations that create an atmosphere of divine splendor. Église de la Madeleine is known for its rich musical tradition, hosting concerts and performances that showcase the church's exceptional acoustics. The church's history is intertwined with the city's cultural and social fabric, serving as a place of worship, commemoration, and inspiration. Whether you seek a moment of quiet contemplation, a glimpse into Parisian history, or a cultural experience through music, Église de la Madeleine invites you to immerse yourself in its timeless beauty and spiritual ambiance.
tagline: A Testament of Divine Elegance: Église de la Madeleine, Paris

title: Bateaux Parisiens
description:
From just behind the Eiffel Tower (which you'll probably be seeing up close anyway), the Bateaux Parisiens river cruises depart for trips up and down the Seine. With a sightseeing option as well as a trés elegant dinner cruise, you'll be able to find the trip to suit your taste.
Enjoy panoramic views of the Musée d'Orsay, Notre-Dame Cathedral, Pont Neuf, and many more UNESCO World Heritage Sites as you float down the Seine. It all starts here.

title: Parc Floral de Paris
tagline: Unwind the city grind with Parisian flower power!

title: Champs-Élysées
description: Champs-Élysées, known as "the most beautiful avenue in the world," is an iconic destination in Paris that exudes elegance, luxury, and charm. Stretching from the Arc de Triomphe to the Place de la Concorde, this grand boulevard is lined with magnificent buildings, high-end boutiques, cafes, theaters, and bustling activity. As you stroll along the Champs-Élysées, you'll be immersed in a vibrant atmosphere, surrounded by renowned fashion houses, exquisite dining establishments, and cultural landmarks. Explore the elegant shops, offering the latest trends and designer labels, or indulge in a delightful café experience, savoring French pastries and gourmet cuisine. Take in the beauty of the tree-lined promenade, admire the majestic Arc de Triomphe, and revel in the energy of this bustling avenue. Champs-Élysées is also home to annual events, such as the Bastille Day parade and the final stage of the Tour de France.
tagline: Where Elegance Meets Glamour: Champs-Élysées, Paris

title: Tuileries Garden
description: Welcome to the Tuileries Garden, an enchanting oasis in the heart of Paris, situated between the Louvre Museum and Place de la Concorde. Greeted by manicured lawns and vibrant flowerbeds, you'll be invited to explore elegant pathways. Find serenity by the fountains or on a cozy bench, escaping the city's bustle. With a rich history dating back to the 16th century, the garden boasts statues, sculptures, and captivating views of landmarks like the Louvre and Eiffel Tower. A cultural hub, it hosts art exhibitions, events, and concerts. Embrace the timeless charm and tranquility of this beloved destination in Paris.
tagline: A Tranquil Haven in the Heart of Paris: Tuileries Garden

title: Le Marais
tagline: Visit the Pomipidou Center, the Victor Hugo Museum and the LGBTQ+ scene

title: Luxembourg Gardens
description: Nestled in the historic Latin Quarter, Luxembourg Gardens is a true gem that combines natural beauty, architectural elegance, and a rich cultural heritage. As you enter the gardens, you'll be greeted by meticulously manicured lawns, vibrant flowerbeds, and peaceful tree-lined pathways. Take a leisurely stroll through the garden, discovering hidden corners, charming statues, and ornate fountains. The iconic Medici Fountain serves as a centerpiece, adding a touch of enchantment to the serene ambiance. Find a comfortable chair or bench to relax and immerse yourself in the tranquility that surrounds you. Luxembourg Gardens also offers various recreational activities, including tennis courts, a puppet theater, and a children's playground, making it a beloved destination for families and visitors of all ages. The garden is an ideal place to enjoy a picnic, read a book, or simply savor the beauty of nature.
tagline: Where Beauty and Serenity Flourish: Luxembourg Gardens, Paris

title: Montmartre
tagline: Climb to the highest point of Montmartre and go to Basilique du Sacré-Cœur

title: Tomb of Napoleon Bonaparte
tagline: Pay homage to France's most mighty, fun-sized emperor

title: Palais Royal
description: Welcome to the Domaine National du Palais-Royal, a historic and refined destination nestled in the heart of Paris. This grand palace complex, encompassing both gardens and buildings, showcases architectural splendor and cultural significance. As you enter the domain, you'll be captivated by the majestic Palais-Royal, with its elegant facade and rich history dating back to the 17th century. Explore the beautifully manicured gardens, adorned with perfectly aligned trees, flowerbeds, and tranquil fountains. Stroll along the colonnaded arcades, known as the Galerie de Montpensier and the Galerie de Valois, which house luxury shops, art galleries, and chic cafes. Immerse yourself in the rich heritage and refined atmosphere, where history, art, and beauty converge to create an unforgettable experience in the heart of Paris.
tagline: Where Heritage Meets Elegance: Domaine National du Palais-Royal, Paris

title: Place de la Concorde
description: Welcome to Place de la Concorde, a grand and historic square located at the heart of Paris. Spanning over 18 acres, Place de la Concorde is one of the most iconic landmarks in the city. As you stand in the square, you'll be surrounded by architectural splendor and rich historical significance. Admire the majestic Obelisk of Luxor, standing tall in the center of the square, offering a link to ancient Egypt. Marvel at the stunning fountains, including the famous Fontaines de la Concorde, which add a touch of elegance to the square. The square is also flanked by notable buildings, such as the Hôtel de Crillon and the Palais Bourbon. Place de la Concorde holds a significant place in French history, being the site of many important events, including the French Revolution and the execution of King Louis XVI. Today, the square serves as a vibrant gathering place, where locals and visitors come to enjoy the beauty of the surroundings, take leisurely walks, or simply relax on the benches.
tagline: Where History and Elegance Converge: Place de la Concorde, Paris

title: Pont Alexandre III
description: As you walk across this iconic bridge, you'll be captivated by its ornate design, intricate details, and breathtaking views of the surrounding landmarks. Admire the majestic sculptures, golden accents, and elegant lamp posts that adorn the bridge, creating an atmosphere of timeless beauty. Pont Alexandre III connects the Invalides and Champs-Élysées districts, serving as a gateway to some of the city's most iconic sites. From the bridge, you can marvel at the magnificent view of the Eiffel Tower, the Dome des Invalides, and the Grand Palais. It's a perfect spot for a romantic stroll, a photo opportunity, or simply to soak in the romantic ambiance that permeates the bridge. Whether you're crossing the bridge during the day or at night when it's illuminated, Pont Alexandre III promises an enchanting experience that captures the essence of Parisian charm and sophistication.
tagline: Where Elegance Meets the River: Pont Alexandre III, Paris

title: Place des Vosges
tagline: Visit one of the oldest squares in Paris and the Victor Hugo Museum

title: Pont Neuf
description: Explore the iconic Pont Neuf in Paris, the oldest standing bridge over the Seine River. Admire its architectural beauty, take a leisurely stroll, and enjoy stunning views of the city's landmarks.
tagline: Discover the timeless beauty of Pont Neuf in Paris

title: Vedettes de Paris
description: This Seine River cruise lets you see the French capital from the deck of a boat, with a glass of Champagne in your hand. The departure point is just behind the Eiffel Tower, and the full length of the tour will bring you up close to some of the city's best landmarks – including the Louvre, Pont Neuf, the Notre-Dame Cathedral, and more.

title: Crazy Horse Paris
description: Crazy Horse is one of the original Parisian cabarets. It was founded in 1951 and features a famously intimate theater. It’s been updated for the 21st century and adds a pop-culture twist on the classic cabaret – so much so that Beyoncé emulated one of their numbers in the music video for Partition (and filmed the video here!). Add in options to enjoy champagne and canapés, and you have yourself a Parisian night-out par excellence!

title: The Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Paris
description: The stunning Basilique du Sacré-Cœur stands atop Montmartre at the highest point in the City of Lights. Built of white Travertine stone, this minor basilica stands as a monument to conservative moral order, right in the heart of Paris's most rebellious neighborhood. It's a delicious contrast.
tagline: Look at the Christ in Majesty, one of the world's largest mosaics and one of heaviest bells, the Savoyarde

title: La Villette
description: The Parc de la Villette is the third-largest park in Paris and is a huge family attraction, especially in the summer months. The park covers 55.5 hectares of the northeastern edge of the city, in the 19th arrondissement.
tagline: Go for a stroll in Parc de la Villette, one of the largest parks in Paris

title: Bateaux Mouches
description: Bateaux Mouches is a river cruise company that invites visitors aboard to explore Paris from its best avenue, the Seine. They are located between the Pont de l'Alma and the Pont des Invalides on the north side of the Seine.

title: Moulin Rouge
description: The Moulin Rouge is an iconic French cabaret located in Paris. Founded in 1889, the iconic windmill-topped building had to be rebuilt in 1921 after a fire destroyed the original premises. Now, the Moulin Rouge houses the resident stage show Féerie which is performed daily to audiences from around the world.

title: AccroCamp: Boucles de Seine
description: Hidden in the trees of charming Moisson is a realm of climbing, clambering adventure. AccroCamp Boucles de Seine has a whole range of rope swings, bridges, climbing walls, and other challenging obstacles for you to navigate. The only thing you need? Bravery... and closed shoes!

title: Saint-Germain-des-Prés
tagline: Learn about the 6th district's intellectual and artistic legacy, or grab coffee at the iconic Café de Flore

title: Paris Balloon Flights
description: Experience the magic of Paris from a whole new perspective! Take a hot air balloon ride and soar above the City of Light. Enjoy breathtaking views of the city’s famous landmarks, winding rivers and romantic landscapes. Feel the wind in your hair as you drift above the city skyline, creating an unforgettable memory that will last a lifetime. Get ready to be amazed!
tagline: Soar above Paris for a memory that lasts a lifetime

title: Paris Segways Tours
description:
Discover the City of Lights like you've never seen it before, with an exciting Segway tour! Get up close and personal with all Paris has to offer and explore the city like a local. Enjoy the iconic landmarks and picturesque scenery, immerse yourself in the culture and let the history of the city wash over you. 
With Segway tours, you can enjoy the best of Paris without having to worry about navigating the metro system. So what are you waiting for? Hop on a Segway and explore the city of Paris!
tagline: Explore Paris like a boss and hop on a Segway!

title: Wine Days Trips from Paris
description: Escape the city and indulge in a wine-lover's paradise on a day trip from Paris to the stunning French countryside. Discover the art of winemaking, savor delicious wines and cheeses, and soak up the picturesque scenery.
tagline: Sip, savor, and relax on our wine day trips from Paris

title: Burgundy Day Trips from Paris
description:
Escape the Parisian hustle and embark on a vibrant journey to Burgundy, a region overflowing with rich history and rustic charm. 
Experience the thrill of dipping into centuries old cellars, walking among verdant vineyards, and indulging in world-renowned wines within this unique rustic treasure. 
Throughout this unforgettable day trip, let the magic of Burgundy - from its storied medieval past to its exquisite culinary traditions - unfold in a symphony of unforgettable experiences.
tagline: Escape to Burgundy for history, charm and wine

title: Champ de Mars
description: Stretching between the Eiffel Tower and École Militaire, Champ de Mars offers a picturesque setting for leisure, relaxation, and breathtaking views. As you enter the park, you'll be greeted by expansive lawns, tree-lined avenues, and well-manicured gardens. Take a leisurely stroll along the pathways, enjoy a picnic on the grass, or simply find a quiet spot to soak in the beauty of the surroundings. The highlight of Champ de Mars is undoubtedly the majestic Eiffel Tower, which stands tall at one end of the park. Admire this iconic landmark from different vantage points within the park, capturing memorable photographs and appreciating its grandeur up close. Champ de Mars is a gathering place for locals and visitors alike, who come to enjoy the outdoors, play games, and take in the romantic atmosphere of Paris. Whether you're seeking a peaceful retreat or a place to bask in the beauty of the Eiffel Tower, Champ de Mars offers a delightful experience for all.
tagline: Where Nature and Iconic Landmarks Meet: Champ de Mars, Paris

title: Trocadéro Gardens
description: Welcome to the Trocadéro Gardens, a magnificent green space located in the 16th arrondissement of Paris. Situated across from the Eiffel Tower, the Trocadéro Gardens offer stunning views of this iconic landmark, making it one of the most picturesque spots in the city. As you enter the gardens, you'll be greeted by beautifully manicured lawns, elegant fountains, and vibrant flowerbeds. Take a leisurely stroll along the pathways, lined with statues and sculptures, and discover peaceful corners to sit and enjoy the serene ambiance. The gardens are a popular gathering place for locals and tourists alike, who come to admire the Eiffel Tower, take memorable photographs, and soak in the beauty of the surrounding architecture. Whether you're seeking a quiet retreat, a romantic setting, or simply a place to appreciate the beauty of Paris, the Trocadéro Gardens offer an enchanting experience in the heart of the city.
tagline: Where Beauty Meets Iconic Views: Trocadéro Gardens, Paris

title: Pont des Arts
description: Welcome to Pont des Arts, a romantic and iconic bridge that spans the Seine River in the heart of Paris. Known as the "Bridge of Arts," Pont des Arts exudes a timeless charm and has become a symbol of love and inspiration. As you step onto the bridge, you'll be captivated by its elegant design and the breathtaking views of the river and the surrounding landmarks. The bridge is famous for its "love locks," padlocks left by couples as a symbol of their eternal love and commitment. Take a leisurely stroll across Pont des Arts, hand in hand with your loved one, and savor the romantic ambiance that fills the air. Enjoy the sounds of street musicians and artists, who often gather on the bridge, adding to its artistic allure. Pont des Arts is a place where emotions run deep, and where the magic of Paris comes alive.
tagline: Where Romance Blossoms: Pont des Arts, Paris

title: Place du Tertre
description: Welcome to Place du Tertre, a vibrant and charming square nestled in the heart of Montmartre, Paris. Known as the artistic hub of the city, this lively square has been a gathering place for artists, musicians, and performers for centuries. As you enter Place du Tertre, you'll be surrounded by cobblestone streets, quaint cafes, and bustling art stalls. The square is renowned for its vibrant atmosphere and the talented artists who set up their easels to create beautiful paintings and portraits. Stroll through the square, admire the artistry on display, and perhaps even have your own portrait done by a skilled artist. The ambiance of Place du Tertre is further enhanced by the aroma of freshly brewed coffee and delicious pastries wafting from the nearby cafes. Immerse yourself in the creative energy, enjoy the live music performances, and embrace the bohemian spirit that permeates this artistic haven.
tagline: Where Art Comes to Life: Place du Tertre, Paris

title: Parc des Buttes Chaumont
description: Welcome to Parc des Buttes Chaumont, a hidden gem nestled in the heart of Paris. This picturesque park offers a peaceful and enchanting escape from the bustling city streets. As you enter Parc des Buttes Chaumont, you'll be greeted by rolling hills, lush greenery, and scenic pathways that wind their way around a central lake. Explore the park's diverse landscapes, from rocky cliffs and waterfalls to charming bridges and secluded groves. Admire the Temple de la Sibylle, a striking architectural feature perched on a rocky island in the middle of the lake. Take a leisurely stroll along the paths, find a cozy spot to have a picnic, or simply sit back and enjoy the tranquility of this natural oasis. Parc des Buttes Chaumont is a beloved destination for nature lovers, joggers, and families, offering breathtaking views, peaceful ambiance, and a respite from the urban pace of Paris.
tagline: Nature's Serenity in the Heart of Paris: Parc des Buttes Chaumont
