title: Food Tours in Brooklyn
description: Available activities span several guided food tours in Brooklyn, showcasing the borough's diverse culinary landscape. Various packages combine tastings at local eateries, markets, and specialty shops, allowing exploration of different neighborhoods and their unique flavors. Tours can be crafted to focus on specific cuisines or culinary traditions, and options for private tours enhance the personal interaction with knowledgeable guides. Participants can expect to sample a variety of dishes while learning about the local food culture and the stories behind famous Brooklyn dishes.
tagline: Join guided tours and sample local cuisine

title: Walking Tours in Brooklyn
description: Various walking tours in Brooklyn present an engaging way to learn about the borough's history, culture, and neighborhoods. Choose from guided adventures that highlight local landmarks, art installations, or food scenes. Options include private tours for a more personalized activity or themed excursions focusing on specific interests like street art, architecture, or historical narratives. Tours often incorporate storytelling, local insights, and stops at notable sites, allowing participants to interact with the community and gain a deeper appreciation of Brooklyn's unique character.
tagline: Join guided tours and private tours

title: Amsterdam Public Transport Tickets
description:
Experience the beauty of Amsterdam with the ease of public transport. 
With the city's comprehensive network of trams, metros, and buses, you can explore Amsterdam from every angle. By securing your tickets in advance, you can get to the city's hot spots spots quickly and safely.
Don't worry about having to find the right ticket at the station, or on the tram; make your time in the Dutch capital as easy as possible by picking up your Amsterdam Public transport tickets ahead of time.
tagline: Explore Amsterdam with ease – get your public transport ticket now!

title: Embassy of the Free Mind
description: Explore a collection of ancient manuscripts and rare books. Attend lectures and discussions on philosophy and spirituality.
tagline: View ancient manuscripts and attend lectures on philosophy

title: Amsterdam Canal Cruises
description:
The best way to enjoy Amsterdam is through its most unique feature – the canals. Choose from an array of Amsterdam canal cruises and soak up the city's history, architecture, and fascinating views!
Amsterdam boat tours offer a range of experiences for guests of all ages and interests. Choose from a morning canal cruise served with breakfast, or a leisurely day tour with your choice of pizza, BBQ, or Gouda cheese tasting. Let the captain do the steering while you enjoy a drink (or two) on a booze cruise, or book a romantic evening excursion instead. There are plenty of options!
Get to know Amsterdam with an expert guide to point out historic points of interest, showcase classic photography opportunities, and share fun facts about the city. Canal tours are available in several languages and most offer audio guides. Rain or shine, these semi-covered tour boats are your ticket to experience this iconic city in true Dutch fashion.
tagline: Navigate Amsterdam’s historic waterways on a picturesque canal cruise

title: Amsterdam Schiphol Airport Transfers
description: Enjoy easer transfers between Amsterdam Schiphol Airport and locations in and around Amsterdam.
tagline: Travel between Amsterdam Schiphol Airport and Amsterdam or its surrounding areas.

title: Van Gogh Museum
description:
The Van Gogh Museum displays over 200 of Vincent van Gogh's works, including landscapes, still lifes and drawings, and letters. By displaying them alongside works from Van Gogh's contemporaries, viewers get real insights into his life and work.
The museum itself is comprised of two buildings. The main building, designed by Gerrit Rietveld, Joan van Dillen, and J. van Tricht, opened in 1973. The second building, designed by Japanese architect Kisho Kurokawa was added in 1999. Located in Amsterdam's Museumplein (along with the Stedelijk and Rijksmuseum), the Van Gogh Museum is one of the most-visited museums in the Netherlands.
Book Van Gogh Museum tickets and get up close to masterpieces like "The Potato Eaters," "Almond Blossom," "Two Cut Sunflowers", and "Irises," not to mention one or two of those timelessly intense self portraits!
tagline: Immerse yourself in Van Gogh's vivid colors, textured brushstrokes, and poignant expressions

title: Moco Museum Amsterdam
description:
The Modern Contemporary (Moco) Museum Amsterdam is housed inside Villa Alsberg, a 1904 townhouse in the middle of Amsterdam Museumplein. 
High-profile guest curators and exhibitions highlight some of the modern world's best contributors to the art world. Eduard Cuypers, cousin of Rijksmuseum designer Pierre Cuypers, designed the townhouse, making it a sight to see in itself. 
But the work inside is absolutely phenomenal; two current attractions showcase the work of street art activist Banksy, and pop artist extraordinaire, Andy Warhol.
tagline: Banksy, Andy Warhol and more under one roof at Moco Museum Amsterdam

title: Amsterdam City Tours
description:
Explore the beauty of Amsterdam on a city tour! Discover the city's vibrant culture, architecture, and history while taking in the sights and sounds of the Dutch capital. 
With knowledgeable and experienced tour guides, you are sure to get the most out of your Amsterdam experience. Get ready to discover the historic canal ring with its quaint bridges, as well as hidden gems you could easily walk past.
There's also a range of self-guided audio tour option so you can enjoy Amsterdam at your own pace.
tagline: Unlock the secrets of Amsterdam on a city tour

title: Efteling
description:
Originally opened in 1952 as a fairytale forest with animatronic characters, Efteling has grown in size and stature to become one of Europe's premier themeparks. 
With rides, restaurants, and attractions, visitors will be immersed in a magical forest full of elves and fairies from Dutch folklore.
tagline: Wander through an enchanted amusement park in the Netherlands

title: Madame Tussauds Amsterdam
description:
Madame Tussauds is located in the center of Amsterdam at the ever-busy Dam Square. Founded by wax sculptor Marie Tussaud in the 18th century, this iconic wax museum chain has locations in many cities across the globe, including London, Berlin, New York, Las Vegas, Hong Kong, and Shanghai, and of course, the Dutch capital. 
Madame Tussauds Amsterdam is home to hundreds of historical and royal figures, film and sports stars, and immersive themed galleries. The museum encourages an interactive approach to the wax figures – so go ahead and hug your favorite celebrity crush! Madame Tussauds has been a central fixture in Amsterdam since 1971, with its first location at the Kalverstraat, before being moved to the Dam Square, where it can't be missed – and definitely shouldn't!

title: ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo
description:
ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo is a famous zoo and botanical garden founded in 1838. It is the Netherlands' oldest zoo and one of the oldest in the world, featuring several historic buildings and architectural gems that provide a dose of Dutch culture alongside incredible wildlife encounters.
Located in the heart of the city, ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo covers an area of over 10 hectares and is home to some 700 species of animals, including Asian elephants, African penguins, net giraffes, jaguars, and more. The zoo is also renowned for its botanical gardens containing some of the world's rarest trees and exotic plants.
The zoo has a variety of interactive exhibitions, including a Planetarium, Aquarium, Geological Museum, Insectarium, and a Butterfly Pavilion. There are also many educational and recreational activities for kids, including a petting zoo, playgrounds, and an interactive science center.
tagline: See exotic animals, tour historic buildings and explore the botanical wonders at ARTIS Amsterdam

title: Fabrique des Lumières
description:
See art come to life at Fabrique des Lumières - the largest digital art center in the Netherlands - in the Westergasterrein, Amsterdam.
A large industrial space with walls up to 17 meters high features immersive exhibitions by classic, modern and contemporary artists. With advanced technology, the artworks are brought to life by light projections and accompanying music, as well as a downloadable app that will further enrich your experience.
tagline: See art come to life at the largest digital art center in the Netherlands

title: THIS IS HOLLAND
description:
THIS IS HOLLAND offers a simulated flight experience with a spherical screen and special effects to enhance a state-of-the-art journey over The Netherlands. Visitors will see cultural highlights and landmarks, as well as learn about the history of The Netherlands.
THIS IS HOLLAND is located in Amsterdam and can be reached via ferry from Amsterdam Central Station.

title: NEMO Science Museum
description: NEMO Science Museum is a science centre in Amsterdam, located at the Oosterdok. It was built on top of the IJ-tunnel that connects the centre of Amsterdam with the north of the city. The striking building, designed by Italian architect and engineer, Renzo Piano, opened its doors in 1997. Some say the shape of the building mimics the appearance of a large boat, but others suggest that it's actually a reflection of the tunnel it was built upon.

title: Heineken Experience
description:
The Heineken Experience is located in the original Heineken Brewery. Gerard Adriaan Heineken bought this building back in 1863 when it was the De Hooiberg brewery. He then turned it towards the production of his eponymous beer. The building remained a brewery all the way up to 1988. It was then that it became a museum (and hotspot for Heineken consumption).
Located in the vibrant neighborhood of De Pijp, the Heineken Experience is ideally situated for a canal cruise, and some city exploration that takes you away from the hustle and bustle of Dam Square.
Since 2001, it's been the world-famous Heineken Experience. After a year-long update and redesign in 2008, the Heineken Experience is more Heineken-y and Experience-y than ever before.

title: Keukenhof Day Trip
description:
Discover the beauty of the Netherlands and take a day trip to Keukenhof. You'll be amazed at the colorful and fragrant tulip fields that span the landscape. 
Explore the picturesque paths of the park and take in the unique Dutch culture. With a variety of activities and plenty of photo opportunities, you're sure to have a perfect day in the Dutch countryside.
tagline: Don't miss your chance to see the Keukenhof's tulips in bloom

title: Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam
description: Discover the enchanting city of Amsterdam at your own pace with Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam. Immerse yourself in the rich history, artistic heritage, and vibrant culture of this captivating city. Our self-guided tours provide the freedom to explore Amsterdam's iconic landmarks, charming neighborhoods, and world-class museums. Wander along the picturesque canals, marvel at the masterpieces in the Van Gogh Museum, and soak up the lively atmosphere of Dam Square. With Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam, create your personalized itinerary, follow our expert guides, and uncover the hidden gems that make Amsterdam truly special. Start your self-guided adventure and experience the magic of Amsterdam.
tagline: Unveil the Wonders of Amsterdam with Self-Guided Tours!

title: Rembrandt House Museum
description: View original artworks and personal belongings of Rembrandt. Watch demonstrations of traditional etching and painting techniques.
tagline: Learn about Rembrandt's life and see his original etchings

title: Pancake Boat Amsterdam
description: The Pancake Boat, or Pannenkoekenboot, is a buffet experience in Amsterdam where guests can eat delicious Dutch pancakes while cruising the IJ. The Pancake Boat docks at Amsterdam NDSM, a brief ferry ride away from Centraal Station.

title: Amsterdam City Cards
description:
Experience all the best of Amsterdam with a city card! Save time and money on your sightseeing adventures with an all-in-one pass that gives you access to many of the city's top attractions. 
Discover all the city has to offer with range of convenient and budget-friendly city card. 
Explore the city's canals and historical hotspots, as well as its unmissable museums. With a city card, you're guaranteed to make the most of your time in Amsterdam. Get your city card today and experience what makes Amsterdam so special!
tagline: Make the most of Amsterdam with a city card

title: Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam
description:
The Stedelijk Museum in Amsterdam was founded in 1874 and officially opened its doors in 1895. The building was designed by the architect Adriaan Willem Weissman and is located at Museum Square, where you can also find The Rijksmuseum and The Van Gogh Museum.
From 2004 until 2012 The Stedelijk Museum was undergoing a renovation. The building was extended with an extra wing, this wing is also known as 'The Bathtub.'
tagline: Step into a world of impressive art and design from local and global artists

title: The Cat Cabinet
description:
The Cat Cabinet (Het KattenKabinet) is puuurrfectly located in the 'Golden Bend' of the Herengracht. Situated on the classically-furnished piano nobile of a storied 17th-century canal house, there are many rooms full of cat paraphernalia (and real cats). 
This house (and the one next to it) were once the property of the Van Eeghen family. In 1985 it was bought by the Stichting Herengracht 497 and restored, eventually becoming the feline-filled fantasy it is today.
It's an easy tram or bike ride from most places in Amsterdam.

title: Royal Palace Amsterdam (Koninklijk Paleis)
description:
The impressive Royal Palace Amsterdam, located on Dam Square, is one of Amsterdam's top attractions. It was built in the Dutch Golden Age as the city hall for Amsterdam. It later became a palace for the royal family, a function it still has to this day. Consequently, it's not always open to the public.
It plays a key role during State visits and it's also home to some pretty amazing 17th-century art - representing the Dutch Golden Age.

title: A'DAM Lookout
description:
A'DAM Lookout sits atop A’DAM Toren, the newly refurbished 22-story 'vertical city,' on the north side of the IJ. It offers an unimpeded 360-degree view of Amsterdam from almost 100 meters above ground, on the city’s only publicly accessible observation deck.
The A’DAM Toren is one of the highlights of the transformation of the IJ riverbank.
tagline: Prepare for a impeccable 360-degree view of Amsterdam

title: The Upside Down
description:
The Upside Down is the biggest Instagram museum in Europe – but it's more than just a museum. It's part amusement park, part club, part magic mushroom trip! Get your friends together and remember to charge your phones; you'll need battery power for all the photos you'll take in this mind-melting world of fun. Explore the boundaries of your own creativity, trick your brain and tickle your senses.
The Upside Down is located at Europaboulevard in Amsterdam, just a short tram ride or cycle from central Amsterdam.

title: Rijksmuseum
description:
The National Museum of the Netherlands, or the Rijksmuseum, has been housed on this location since 1885. It recently underwent a ten-year, €375 million renovation. The Rijksmuseum has over 5,000 paintings (including Rembrandt's Night Watch) and has an emphasis on the Dutch Golden Age. But it's not just paintings – there are sculptures, antiques, collections of clothing and weapons, and one of the finest actively-used libraries you'll ever see. 
The Dutch architect P.J.H. Cuypers designed this elegant building, and even put himself in the design as one of the statues on the roof.
tagline: Journey through Dutch art history, from Rembrandt to Van Gogh, in Amsterdam

title: The Amsterdam Dungeon
description:
The Amsterdam Dungeon first opened its doors to the public of Amsterdam in 2006 and has been scaring people ever since. The attraction is located inside an historical church and neo-gothic elements of the architecture date back to 1912. You can even see them in some scenes of the show!
The Dungeon is located right in central Amsterdam and can be reached easily on foot or by bike.
The Amsterdam Dungeon is a spooktacular journey into the darkside of the city's 500-year-old history. It's right in the city center.

title: Aloha Amsterdam
description: Aloha is a Hawaiian-themed entertainment complex in Amsterdam featuring a full bowling alley, a glow-in-the-dark minigolf course, a laser tag arena, and a grill restaurant and bar. It's the ideal spot for friends and families looking to enjoy a bit of old-fashioned fun in the Dutch capital.

title: Red Light Secrets Museum
description: The Red Light Secrets Museum, or Amsterdam's Museum of Prostitution, is a window into what goes on in the famous (or notorious?) Red Light District. Located at Oudezijds Achterburgwal 60H, the museum is just a 5-minute walk from Centraal Station, on one of the picturesque canals that make up the area the locals call 'de Wallen'.
tagline: Learn about the Red Light District and see historic canal views

title: Muiderslot Castle
description: Muiderslot Castle is a defensive castle built in the middle ages, just outside of Amsterdam, North Holland. It is now a museum housing relics of the medieval era, as well as its famous residents like Pieter Corneliszoon Hooft. It is roughly 20 kilometers south east of Amsterdam and can be reached via public transport, or bike, in roughly an hour.

title: Wereldmuseum Amsterdam
description:
The beautiful and historic building in which the Wereldmuseum Amsterdam is housed is a little out of the main tourist areas. It's in leafy Amsterdam Oost, not far from the Artis Zoo. 
Formerly the Tropenmuseum, this museum is dedicated to other cultures and countries, and is one of Amsterdam's hidden treasures.
tagline: Learn about cultures from all over the world without leaving Amsterdam

title: ARTIS Micropia
description: Micropia is a museum dedicated to the microscopic life of microbes. It is located beside Amsterdam's ARTIS Zoo. It is located east of the Amstel and is easily accessible by tram, or ten minutes' walk from Waterlooplein metro station.

title: Foam Photography Museum Amsterdam
description: This building on the Keizersgracht was originally built in 1685. In the 19th century it was already in use as a museum. First by local Amsterdam artists, it's currently Amsterdam's renowned Photography Museum.

title: Museum Our Lord in the Attic
description:
This 17th-century canal house has a literal highlight - a miniature, yet fully functional, church hidden in its attic!
Now Amsterdam's oldest museum, Ons' Lieve Heer Op Solder is a beautiful slice of perfectly preserved history and exemplifies the city's tolerant attitude towards culture and religion.

title: Amaze Amsterdam
description:
AMAZE Amsterdam is an immersive experience created by the world's largest dance event organizer ID&T. Visitors experience a journey from head to heart, where they are taken through seven experience worlds filled with state-of-the-art audiovisual technology.
A labyrinth of storytelling is on offer, with shows full of lasers, lights, visuals, 11D sound, interactive effects and especially sound and music. AMAZE combines 30 years of show experience into a new, exciting way of going out – the next phase in the evolution of entertainment.

title: Barbie - The Dream Experience
description: Explore life-sized versions of iconic Barbie toys and famous scenes. Attend interactive exhibits and take photos with iconic dolls.
tagline: Step into a Barbie world and discover life-sized replicas of Barbie toys

title: Hortus Botanicus
description: Walk through different themed gardens and observe unique plant species. Enjoy the peaceful atmosphere and attend educational workshops.
tagline: Visit the greenhouses and walk through the butterfly garden

title: WONDR Experience
description: WONDR Experience is an immersive experience for adults and kids alike (but mostly adults!) that stimulates the senses with candy-colored dive-in pools filled with marshmallows and more. It is located in Amsterdam North, less than 10 minutes away from Amsterdam Central Station.
tagline: Enjoy candy-colored dive-in pools filled with marshmallows and more

title: Zaanse Schans & Volendam Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Escape the hustle-bustle of Amsterdam for a day and take a scenic trip to the quaint towns of Zaanse Schans and Volendam. 
Get the complete Dutch experience with a visit to the historic windmills located in Zaanse Schans and the delicious cheese factories which offer free tastings!
Also visit the old Dutch town of Volendam, with its colorful wooden houses and fishing boats in the harbor, your day is bound to be exciting.
tagline: Take a break from the crowds of Amsterdam and take a fun day trip to Zaanse Schans and Volendam

title: Museum of The Canals
description:
In a monumental (and gorgeous!) building on the Herengracht, lies the Museum of the Canals (Grachtenmuseum). In this museum you'll develop a deeper appreciation for the Dutch capital - and the waterways that are such an important part of this city's identity. 
This museum uses a broad set of tools - holograms, paintings, models, audio guides, and architecture (among others) to bring the significance and experience of Amsterdam's famous canals to life.
tagline: Learn more about Amsterdam's UNESCO-protected canals

title: Amsterdam Winter Paradise
description:
Check out one of the best Amsterdam winter activities at Winter Paradise, hosted at RAI Amsterdam. 
The convention center turns into a wintery wonderland with a festive Ferris wheel, warm mulled wine, and ice skating. 
A frosty festival awaits after dusk including drinks, dancing, and live music.
tagline: Celebrate the holiday season from December to January with drinks, dance and music

title: Amsterdam Hop-on Hop-off Tours
description:
Experience Amsterdam in the most convenient way possible with a Hop-on Hop-off tour of the city! 
These experiences allow you to explore Amsterdam with ease – simply hop on one of the many buses, or boats, and explore the city at your own pace. 
Take the stress out of your trip to Amsterdam and enjoy quick and easy transportation to the city's main sights. Each Hop-on Hop-off tour includes a sightseeing route with plenty of information, as well as convenient stops at top attractions where you can hop off!
tagline: Discover Amsterdam with a Hop-on Hop-off tour of the city

title: Nationaal Holocaustmuseum
description:
Once a thriving theatre before World War II, the Hollandsche Schouwburg tragically became a Nazi collection point for the deportation of Jews from July 1942 onward. 
Thousands were detained here for hours, days, or even weeks before being dispatched to concentration and extermination camps. Today, the former theatre stands as a poignant memorial, honoring all victims of the Holocaust.
tagline: Take time to reflect, honor, and learn about the victims of the Holocaust

title: Amsterdam Light Festival Cruises
description:
The Amsterdam Light Festival transforms the city's iconic canals into a sparkling winter playground, filled with illuminated artworks from some of the world's top artists. 
Of course, the best way to experience the dazzling artistry is from the water. This selection of cruises lets you get up close and personal with the best festival from the UNESCO-protected canal network.
tagline: See illuminated art installations from the water

title: Van Gogh & Rembrandt in Amsterdam
description:
Explore the captivating "Van Gogh & Rembrandt in Amsterdam" show, where the works of these two master artists are showcased in an immersive sound and light show inside the historic Noorderkerk.
Learn how Vincent van Gogh was inspired by Rembrandt during his time in Amsterdam. Get closer to the art and see how Rembrandt's work guided Van Gogh's pieces. Feel the narrative brought to life through readings of Van Gogh's letters to his brother as you watch the church walls transform into a 360° canvas.
tagline: Experience Van Gogh's work and uncover Rembrandt's influence at this immersive show!

title: Amsterdam Food Tours
description:
Explore Amsterdam's delicious side through a food tour of the city! Sample traditional Dutch snacks, visit the best markets, and delve into the city's culinary heritage. 
Learn about the unique flavors, recipes, and local ingredients that make Amsterdam's food scene so special. Prepare to bite into bitterballen, savour the sweet taste of stroopwafels, and find out if you can handle raw herring. 
If you have a sweet tooth, then don't miss out on a pancake cooking class.
tagline: Delight your senses on an Amsterdam food tour!

title: Giethoorn Daytrips from Amsterdam
description:
Explore the stunning Dutch village of Giethoorn on a day trip from Amsterdam! 
Discover the unique beauty of this traditional village, where canals and waterways take the place of roads. Enjoy a relaxing boat tour, explore the charming streets and take in the breathtaking natural surroundings.
tagline: Discover the beauty of Giethoorn, the Venice of the north

title: The New Church Amsterdam
description: Located in Amsterdam's Dam Square, De Nieuwe Kerk is a historic monument known for its exhibitions of art, photography, and diverse cultures. With 250,000 visitors annually, it's a popular cultural hub hosting royal ceremonies and cultural events, embodying Dutch culture and history.
tagline: Discover a monument known for its exhibitions of art, photography and diverse cultures

title: Dutch Resistance Museum
description: The Dutch Resistance Museum explores the period from 14 May 1940 to 5 May 1945, when the Netherlands was occupied by Nazi Germany. Visitors can learn about the Dutch way of life via a series of exhibitions featuring artifacts, objects, video displays, and more.

title: Nxt Museum
description: Nxt Museum is the home of new media art in the Netherlands, located in Amsterdam North. Nxt Museum specializes in large-scale, multi-sensory installations fusing technology and artistic expression.

title: Belgium Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Leave the charismatic Dutch canals behind and experience the vibrancy of Belgium, just a stone's throw away from Amsterdam! 
Picture gazing at exquisite architecture, savoring rich chocolates, and engaging with historical narratives woven into the fabric of Belgium's towns and cities. 
Every moment of these day trips teems with beauty and intrigue, promising a memorable adventure across the border!
tagline: Take in the rich history and culture of Belgium

title: Museum Het Schip
description: Museum Het Schip is located in a social housing complex called The Ship, and is one of the most famous and iconic buildings of the Amsterdam School. It is easy to reach via public transport from all areas of Amsterdam.

title: BODY WORLDS - The Happiness Project
description:
The German anatomist Gunther von Hagens, who invented the plastination technique in the late 1970s at teh University of Heidelberg, developed this exhibition.
The Body Worlds Exhibition was first presented in 1995. Showing whole bodies plastinated in lifelike poses and dissected to show various structures and systems of human anatomy, which has since met with public interest and controversy in more than 50 museums and venues around the world.

title: Jewish Cultural Quarter
description:
The Jewish Cultural Quarter includes the Jewish Museum (formerly known as the Jewish Historical Museum), the Portuguese Synagogue (built in 1675), the Hollandsche Schouwburg (National Holocaust Memorial), and the Jewish Museum Junior (an educational children's museum designed like a traditional Jewish home).
The Jewish Museum itself is housed in four monumental synagogues near Waterlooplein, making it a great place to start on a family day out in central Amsterdam.

title: Eye Film Museum
description: Eye Filmmuseum is the museum for film and the art of the moving image. Located in the iconic building on the IJ River, Eye organizes exhibitions at the intersection of film and visual arts and film programs that shed light on the very earliest film history, to the most current social developments. Eye's collection includes more than 50,000 Dutch and international films and a multitude of posters, photographs, film equipment, books and personal archives, including the renowned Unesco World Heritage Collection of film pioneer Jean Desmet. Eye preserves, restores and presents film; from classics, blockbusters and early avant-garde films to the very latest VR experiments.

title: Banksy Museum Amsterdam
description: View a wide range of street art and graffiti. Attend exhibitions showcasing works by different artists.
tagline: View street art and learn about Banksy's techniques

title: Amsterdam Tulip Museum
description: Learn about the history of tulips and their impact on Dutch culture. View a variety of tulip-related artifacts and exhibits.
tagline: Learn about tulip history and view exhibits on tulip cultivation

title: Ripley's Believe It or Not! Amsterdam
description: Located on Amsterdam’s Dam Square, this cabinet of curiosities showcases more than 500 exhibits and artifacts in its five floors. The items, ranging from actual Olympic torches to a very unusual Fabergé egg, are organized in 19 themed galleries. There’s also a spacious café with a view to the square and a 5D cinema on the fourth floor.

title: Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum
description: The world's oldest cannabis museum can be found right in the heart of Amsterdam's Red Light District. Learn all about the history and uses of this popular and controversial plant.

title: XtraCold Icebar Amsterdam
description: Welcome to the coolest bar in Amsterdam (literally). This teeth-chattering locale is the perfect spot to sample the lifestyle of a Polar explorer, without the risk of frostbite. Just meters from the busy Rembrandt Square, this is a great stop on a night on the town in Amsterdam. Free drinks and thermals should keep you warm. The bar is sculpted from 60 tons of natural ice and even your glass here is made from frozen H2O!

title: Amsterdam Boat Rental
description:
Explore Amsterdam in a unique way and rent a boat to cruise around the canals! 
Take in the beauty of the Dutch capital from a fresh perspective, and make sure to bring your camera for the perfect photo opportunity. 
Enjoy the convenience of a self-guided tour and discover the historic and modern architecture, the vibrant culture, and the bustling activity of Amsterdam by renting a boat. With an easy booking process and flexible rentals, you can create a memorable experience in no time!
tagline: Make a splash and cruise Amsterdam in style!

title: Boom Chicago
description:
Located a clog's throw from the center of Amsterdam, Boom Chicago is arguably the holy grail of improv comedy in mainland Europe. 
Founded by three Americans in 1993, this international creative comedy group has launched the careers of comedic superstars like Seth Meyers, Jordan Peele, Amber Ruffin, Kay Cannon (Pitch Perfect), Heather Anne Campbell (Rick & Morty), and the creators and stars of Ted Lasso (Jason Sudeikis, Brendan Hunt, and Joe Kelly).
tagline: See the best improv comics perform in Amsterdam

title: H'ART Museum Hermitage Amsterdam
description: Visit the H'ART Museum in Amsterdam, a cultural hub with an impressive collection from antiquity to the present. Located in a historic landmark, it's a must-see destination. Enjoy the dynamic atmosphere, indoor garden, and lively Grand Café offering various activities like concerts, lectures, movie nights, and art education programs. The museum shares its space with the Amsterdam Museum and Museum of the Mind.
tagline: Visit a cultural hub with an impressive collection from antiquity to the present

title: Playhood
description: Join a fun experience at 'The Hood.' This place is great for both kids and adults, where you can pretend to be a fireman, pizza chef, or astronaut. You can also get your play gear at Albert Klein. Each session lasts 1.5 hours, giving you access to the play area and café. You can try specialty coffees and Dutch toasties.
tagline: Step into a world of creative adventure, perfect for all ages

title: Zaanse Schans Tours
description: Situated on the picturesque Zaan River, Zaanse Schans is an open-air museum that features six working windmills, historic houses, and craft shops. Amble about its quaint lanes and you'll find yourself understanding just how the Dutch lived (and prospered!) in bygone centuries. All of that as you see windmills hard at work and clogs being made.

title: Anne Frank Tours
description:
Pass by the house by the Westerkerk where Anne Frank and her family including four other people, evaded the Nazis by hiding in secret rooms at the back of this quaint 17th century canal house. 
It is a moving testament to the resilience of the human spirit, and a melancholy memorial to the young girl who, through her writing, put a very personal and innocent face on the horror of the Holocaust.
tagline: Walk by Anne Frank's home in hiding during the Holocaust, exactly as she left it

title: Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tours
description:
Welcome to Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tours, where the open road becomes your playground. Experience the ultimate freedom of exploring your destination at your own pace, embracing the thrill of spontaneity and uncovering hidden gems along the way. With our self-guided tours, you have the power to create unforgettable memories and embark on unique adventures. 
Whether you crave the scenic countryside or the vibrant city streets, our diverse range of destinations caters to all preferences. From charming villages to iconic landmarks, the choice is yours. Book your Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tour today and unleash the explorer within you.
tagline: Embrace Freedom on the Open Road - Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tours

title: Museumplein Ice Rink
description: Skate on the large ice rink with friends and family. Enjoy warm drinks at nearby cafes after a fun session on the ice.
tagline: Skate on the ice and sip hot chocolate

title: Huis Willet-Holthuysen
description: This house on the Herengracht was built in 1685-87 for then-mayor Jacob Hop. Louisa Willet-Holthuysen, widow of art collector Abraham Willet (1828-1888), vacated the property in 1895, leaving the house and its art collection to the city of Amsterdam.

title: Rotterdam and The Hague Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Take a captivating day-long adventure to Rotterdam and The Hague from Amsterdam! 
Experience the thrilling pulse of Rotterdam's vibrant metropolitan life, then get swept away by the regal charm and grandeur of Dutch political heart, The Hague. 
This delightfully diverse day trip offers a fascinating peek into two entirely unique Dutch worlds.
tagline: There's more to the Netherlands outside of Amsterdam

title: Highlights of Holland
description: Highlights of Holland is an interactive museum that introduces visitors to the Netherlands in just over an hour. Move through ten themed rooms that showcase the country’s art, landscapes, and traditions.
tagline: See well-known Dutch artworks in digital form and walk through a large tulip display

title: Bike Rentals Amsterdam
description:
Amsterdam is a popular destination for tourists, and many people enjoy exploring the city by bicycle. 
We work with multiple suppliers to offer a variety of different bikes available for rent, including both traditional handbrake and electric bikes. Amsterdam is known for its extensive network of bike paths and waterways, making it an ideal destination for a cycling adventure.
tagline: Navigate the streets of Amsterdam like a local

title: Dining Cruises Amsterdam
description:
Let your taste buds drift away along the canals of Amsterdam with any number of tasty dining cruises in the Dutch capital.
Lunch, dinner, drinks – you name it, and there's a way to wine and dine onboard at any time of day.
tagline: Wine and dine along the canals of Amsterdam

title: The Sloten Windmill
description:
The Sloten Windmill is a functioning draining mill. Known in Dutch as the Molen Van Sloten, it is also the only windmill in Amsterdam which is open to the public on a daily basis.
The Sloten Windmill is located on the outskirts of Amsterdam, not too far from Schiphol Airport. It is easily reached by car and public transport.

title: Oude Kerk, Amsterdam
description:
Discover 700 years of Dutch history within the walls of Amsterdam's oldest building – the Oude Kerk (Old Church). 
The Oude Kerk Amsterdam now serves as the city's newest art institute with permanent and temporary exhibitions showcasing the history and culture of Amsterdam.
tagline: Gain insight into the rich culture and history of Amsterdam's Oude Kerk

title: House of Bols
description:
The House of Bols is a stylish combo of the brand new and the very old. Genever is the Dutch spirit from which gin evolved, and here you’ll find out everything about Lucas Bols – the world’s oldest distilled spirit brand and their 38 liqueurs.
It will also introduce you to the more modern world of cocktail making, and the flair is supported by the knowledge that it’s a recent winner of the Dutch Design Award for Best Exhibition and Experience.

title: Henri Willig Store
description: Henri Willig's cheeses are a global phenomenon. The exclusive baby cheese with the colored packages are an essential part of many supermarkets. Goat cheese, organic cheese and all kinds cow cheeses combined with ingredients such as garlic and chili... Willig's cheeses are unique and you'll find them here!

title: Amsterdam Parking
description:
Looking for parking in Amsterdam? Look no further! These options allow you to park with ease and peace of mind, so you can enjoy your time in the city without worrying about where to leave your car. 
These parking garages have been chosen for you based on their location, convenience, and safety, so your car will be in good hands.
Be sure to reserve your parking spot in advance to guarantee a spot and avoid any unnecessary stress.
tagline: Reserve your parking spot in Amsterdam and enjoy a stress-free visit to the city

title: Frida Kahlo Exhibition Amsterdam
description: View a collection of Frida Kahlo's iconic paintings. Learn about her life and artistic journey through detailed exhibits and multimedia displays.
tagline: See her famous paintings and learn about her life

title: Het Scheepvaartmuseum (National Maritime Museum)
description: Het Scheepvaartmuseum has a fascinating collection of historical nautical items, including a replica of the 18th-century cargo ship Amsterdam. The building was founded in 1656 as a storehouse for the Admiralty in Amsterdam.

title: Amsterdam Museum
description: The Amsterdam Museum tells the story of the Dutch capital, from the Middle Ages through the 17th century, and right up to modern times. When it opened its doors in 1926 it was located in the Waag at the Nieuwmarkt. In 1975 the museum moved to its current location, just off the Kalverstraat, a building that was originally a municipal orphange. The collection is full of photos, artifacts and interactive exhibits.
tagline: Learn about Dutch history and see historical artifacts

title: Rembrandts Amsterdam Experience
description: Prepare to step back to the 17th century with Rembrandt Amsterdam Experience. This 5D experience uses music, video projections and special effects to transport you back to Amsterdam as it was when Rembrandt was a citizen of the city, showing a reconstruction of Rembrandt's studio on the Rozengracht canal where he created his final masterpieces.

title: Dutch Pancake Masters
description:
Get to enjoy a variety of classic Dutch pancakes at Dutch Pancake Masters. 
Choose from sweet or savory options, with toppings like fresh berries, crispy bacon, and syrup. A great spot for breakfast, lunch, or a snack. Relax in a cozy setting while you taste the Dutch tradition.
tagline: Savor, relax, and taste delicious Dutch pancakes in a cozy setting

title: Amsterdam Beer Experiences
description:
Get a true taste of Amsterdam with all kinds of beer experiences.
Your personal tour guide will take you to some of the best breweries in the city, giving you an insider's look at the Amsterdam beer scene.
tagline: Explore Amsterdam's beer scene with a tour or tasting

title: Red Light District Amsterdam Tour
description:
Explore Amsterdam's notorious Red Light District on a number of available tours. 
Whether with a guide, or a self-guided visit, you'll learn all about the history and cultural significance of Amsterdam's Red Light District.
tagline: Explore the famous Red Light District in the heart of Amsterdam

title: Johan Cruijff ArenA
description:
Amsterdam's Ajax ArenA is so awesome that they spell it with a capital 'A' at the front and the back of the name. 
It's the largest stadium in the whole country and has a retractable roof. When not being used for Ajax (or national team) games, big ticket concerts are sometimes held here. Artists including Madonna, Michael Jackson, David Bowie, and The Rolling Stones have all played gigs here.

title: Hard Rock Cafe Amsterdam
description: The Hard Rock Cafe Amsterdam is one of the most popular restaurants for visitors to the city. It offers good food and service in a top canal-side location, close to Leidseplein and Vondelpark.

title: Amsterdam Bike Tours
description:
Explore Amsterdam like a local on a bike tour. Cycle your way around the city and get a first-hand understanding of why the Dutch have embraced the bike! 
These pedal-powered tours allow you to experience the real Amsterdam, from its beautiful canals to its bustling markets. With the help of an experienced guide, you'll get to see the city like never before. 
So, hop on a bike, enjoy some fresh air, and experience a tour that you won't soon forget.
tagline: Discover Amsterdam by bike on these pedal-powered tours

title: Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Experience the beauty of the Netherlands, and beyond, with an amazing day trip from Amsterdam! 
Discover the Lowlands' stunning landscapes and charming villages as you journey through the Dutch countryside to your destination. 
There's plenty of day trips to choose from, including relaxing boat rides along the quaint canals of Giethoorn, to informative tours of locations like Volendam and Bruges.
Explore Dutch culture and learn about some of the country's most famous symbols, including windmills and tulip fields. 
Leave Amsterdam for the day and make memories that will last a lifetime.
tagline: Explore the beauty of the Netherlands with an unforgettable day trip from Amsterdam

title: Volendam Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Discover the beauty of the Dutch countryside and take a day trip to Volendam, a small fishing village near Amsterdam that's packed with charm. 
Admire the traditional Dutch architecture, experience traditions that are centuries old, and wander through the quaint streets. While you're visiting, make sure you sample the local cuisine and pick up some Dutch cheese, or maybe even a pair of clogs, to take home!
The culture and history of this unique and picturesque village is sure to fascinate you. You can also take a ride across the Markermeer lake and visit the idyllic former island of Marken. 
Enjoy a day of sightseeing and leisure, and make the most of your visit to the Netherlands!
tagline: Visit Volendam and get a taste of classic Dutch culture

title: Cannabis Museum Amsterdam
description:
Welcome to Cannabis Museum Amsterdam, your premier destination for an immersive and educational journey into the world of cannabis. Located in the vibrant heart of Amsterdam, we take pride in offering an adventurous and interactive experience like no other.
Explore the fascinating history of cannabis, from its diverse uses as an industrial product and superfood to its role as a valuable medicine. Delve into the captivating story of how humanity has domesticated, cultivated, and spread this remarkable plant from its origins on the Central Asian steppes to the iconic coffeeshops of Amsterdam and beyond. Join us for a hands-on exploration, where you’ll discover the rich history, unravel the scientific wonders, and dispel the misconceptions surrounding this extraordinary plant.

title: Amsterdam Walking Tours
description: Discover the real beauty of Amsterdam on foot. Walk past the Anne Frank House, admire the exterior of the Rijksmuseum, walk along the Prinsengracht and Herengracht canals, and see for yourself the atmosphere of the Red Light District.
tagline: Walk through the beautiful streets of the Dutch capital

title: Diamond Museum
description:
The Diamond Museum is Amsterdam's sparkliest attraction, plunging you into the history of the city's diamond trade and showcasing world-famous diamonds from all over the globe.
It's in central Amsterdam and can be easily reached on foot or by bike.

title: The BonTon Tour
description: BonTon Stripclub Amsterdam is the oldest, biggest, and best-rated gentlemen's club that Amsterdam has to offer. It's located right in the heart of Amsterdam, just a 5-minute walk from Leidseplein.

title: Ctaste - Dining in the dark Amsterdam
description:
Want dinner with a difference? Ctaste has what you're looking for, but it might be hard to see. This unique dining experience takes place in total darkness; there isn't a light to be found!
Visually impaired waiters will take your order, serve your meal, and be there to take any extra drink orders. With one of your senses removed, your sense of taste will feel much more intense. So, focus on the textures, indulge in the aromas of your wine, and enjoy.

title: ARTIS Groote Museum
description:
The museum of big questions. From communicating plants to a competition with a chimpanzee, or a smell tunnel where recognition and emotion alternate. Expect a personal expedition as you criss-cross through the museum.
Built between 1850 and 1855 for the members of the Society Natura Artis Magistra, it stands on the oldest grounds of ARTIS and at the time was one of the most modern buildings in Amsterdam. The museum, which closed in 1947, has since been completely renovated and restored.

title: Amsterdam Photoshoots
description:
Make more than just memories with a photo shoot in beautiful Amsterdam. There's no better backdrop than the Dutch capital.
Whether you’re a tourist or a local, the city’s iconic canals, vibrant streets, and historic buildings make it the perfect location for any photo shoot. Join an experienced photographer and capture all the best moments of your trip!
tagline: Capture your time in Amsterdam with a photo shoot

title: Amsterdam Nightlife
description:
Amsterdam is one of Europe's most vibrant cities, and its nightlife is no exception! 
Known around the world for its DJs, it's no surprise that Amsterdam is full of quality clubs. There's also plenty of stylish bars and laid-back pubs, you'll find something to suit every taste. 
Pick up a ticket that gets you access to top bars and clubs, with some drinks secured in advance. You're guaranteed a unique and unforgettable night out in the Dutch capital.
tagline: Make the most of Amsterdam's nightlife and world-class music scene

title: Houseboat Museum Amsterdam
description: Explore the interior of a real houseboat and understand the lifestyle of its inhabitants. View exhibits detailing the history and cultural significance of houseboats in Amsterdam.
tagline: Tour a historic houseboat and learn about life on the water

title: Begijnhof Amsterdam
tagline: Echoes of epic history in the city's beating heart

title: Dam Square
description:
Dam Square is the historical heart of Amsterdam. It is also the place of lots of local and national events. At the Dam Square you can find the Royal Palace, The New Church and The National Monument.
Dam Square is located in the city centre of Amsterdam, near Amsterdam Central Station. The National Monument at the square was built in 1956 as a memoriam of the Second World War victims. Because incredibly beautiful buildings surround the square it is very popular for tourists.
tagline: Vist Amsterdam's most famous square and its historical buildings

title: Red Light District
description:
Amsterdam's Red Light District is one of the oldest and most famous areas of the city. Entwined with the city's seafaring past, the Red Light District hosts some of the city's oldest structures built in the traditional Dutch style. Wander the classic cobblestone streets and admire high church towers – including the Oude Kerk – around every corner.
While the Red Light District is named for the infamous "red lights" that adorn the alleyways, there's so much more to explore. Red Light Secrets and Our Lord in the Attic are some of the museum attractions tucked in between shops, restaurants, and pubs. There are also guided and audio narrated tours of the Red Light District that'll fill you in on the area's fascinating history.

title: Vondelpark
description: Vondelpark, nestled in the heart of Amsterdam, is a picturesque urban oasis that offers a serene escape from the bustling city. Spanning over 120 acres, this iconic park is a beloved gathering place for locals and visitors alike. As you enter Vondelpark, you'll be greeted by lush greenery, tranquil ponds, and winding pathways that invite you to explore its natural beauty. The park is a haven for outdoor enthusiasts, offering opportunities for jogging, cycling, picnicking, and leisurely walks. Admire the beautiful sculptures and charming bridges that dot the landscape, or relax on the grass and soak up the peaceful ambiance. Vondelpark also hosts various cultural events and performances, including open-air concerts at the Vondelpark Openluchttheater. With its spacious lawns, charming cafés, and playgrounds, Vondelpark is a perfect place for families to unwind and children to play.
tagline: A Tranquil Haven in the Heart of Amsterdam: Vondelpark

title: 9 Streets
description:
For a true taste of the Dutch capital, look no further than the "9 Streets" or 9 Straatjes. This area is known for its cozy cafes, unique boutiques, and signature architecture. 
Join a tour to discover the hidden history of the area, or simply explore on your own as you wander over the bridges and canals.
tagline: Discover the heart of Amsterdam in the 9 Streets

title: Skinny Bridge
description: Welcome to the Skinny Bridge, an iconic landmark spanning Amsterdam's Amstel River. Also known as Magere Brug in Dutch, it's a beloved symbol of the city. Enchanting with its slender and elegant design, it adds a touch of romance and nostalgia to the Amsterdam skyline. At night, the bridge's beautiful illumination creates a magical atmosphere along the river. Whether on foot or bike, crossing the bridge offers breathtaking views of the Amstel River and historic buildings. Its unique architectural style against the picturesque backdrop makes it a favorite spot for photography and couples. Legend has it that the bridge's name came from its narrowness, requiring pedestrians to squeeze past each other. Today, the Skinny Bridge stands as a testament to Amsterdam's rich history and serves as a gateway to the city's charming canals and cultural treasures. Immerse yourself in its allure and let it transport you to a bygone era of romance and beauty.
tagline: A Romantic Icon in Amsterdam: The Skinny Bridge

title: Albert Cuyp Markt
description: Browse numerous stalls selling fresh produce, clothing, and souvenirs. Sample a variety of street foods and enjoy the bustling atmosphere.
tagline: Shop for local produce and taste traditional Dutch street food

title: Station Amsterdam Centraal
description:
This station is the biggest railway station in the Netherlands. Because of all the travelers who pass this station, Amsterdam Centraal is the most visited national heritage of the Netherlands. The station offers the possibility to take the train, metro, tram, bus, taxi, boat or bicycle.  
The building itself was built between 1881 and 1889, based on the design of  P.J.H. Cuypers, A.L. van Gendt and L.J. Eijmer. The station has 11 platforms and is located in the city centre of Amsterdam. Therefore the station is the perfect location to start some Amsterdam sightseeing!

title: Leidseplein
tagline: Immerse yourself in Amsterdam's lively party heartbeat

title: Royal Coster Diamonds
description: Royal Coster Diamonds is the oldest diamond polishing factory in the world. Founded in 1840, it still operates to this day, and has handled some of the most famous diamonds in the world – including the Koh i Noor.

title: Jewish History Museum
description:
Jewish history runs through the streets and buildings of Amsterdam. Reminders of the holocaust are ever-present, from the queues outside the Anne Frank House to the brass cobblestones serving as memorial plaques outside the homes of Jewish residents lost during the war.
The Jewish History Museum has gathered a wealth of information to paint a picture of Dutch Jewish life, both as it was in the past and in its current state. The museum also includes a Children's Museum where kids can learn about Jewish life and traditions in a playful way, from learning some Hebrew words to helping bake bread in a Kosher kitchen.

title: Museum Tot Zover
description:
How do we deal with death? That question is central to the Tot Zover Museum. How we deal with death says a lot about who we are, our origins and the times we live in. The Tot Zover Museum provides information about funeral rituals and offers reflection on mortality in a broad sense. 
The museum houses permanent and temporary exhibitions. The collection is of a cultural-historical nature and includes old hair paintings, death masks, post-mortem photography, coffins, urns, but also, for example, a collection of miniature cars and ingredients for a modern Muslim funeral such as lotus powder, camphor and musk.

title: De Tulperij
description:
Step into a world of vibrant colors, intoxicating fragrances, and breathtaking beauty at De Tulperij, the ultimate destination for flower lovers. Nestled in the heart of the picturesque Dutch countryside, this enchanting floral paradise invites you to experience the magic of tulips like never before.
De Tulperij have mastered the art of cultivating and showcasing iconic Dutch tulips, renowned worldwide for their elegance and charm. With meticulous care and a deep-rooted passion for flowers, the expert team nurtures thousands of tulip bulbs, ensuring an awe-inspiring display that will leave you captivated.
Immerse yourself in a kaleidoscope of hues as you wander through meticulously curated fields, bursting with tulips in every shade imaginable. Let the sweet scent of blossoms enchant your senses as you stroll along winding pathways, surrounded by nature's most exquisite artwork.
tagline: Immerse yourself in a kaleidoscope of floral fragrances and hues

title: Amsterdam Helicopter Flights
description:
Experience the thrill of a helicopter flight over Holland! 
Take in the stunning views of the region's tulip fields from above. You'll be dazzled by the range of colors and have a view that others can only dream of.
Book your helicopter flight today and enjoy an unforgettable experience.
tagline: Take to the skies for views that will last a lifetime

title: Amsterdam Bowling
description:
Looking for a fun activity to do with friends and family? How about some time bowling in the heart of Amsterdam! 
Whether you’re used to hitting strikes, or you need guard rails, this experience is guaranteed to bring about some good times. Enjoy some friendly competition, try to beat your previous score, or just have some good times.
tagline: Have you got what it takes to get a strike?

title: Zaanse Schans Day Trips from Amsterdam
description:
Experience the amazing beauty of the Dutch countryside on a daytrip from Amsterdam to the iconic neighborhood of Zaanse Schans. 
See quaint windmills and tranquil canals, or take a step back in time to learn about traditional Dutch life. Discover the unique culture and history of this stunning region and make memories that will last a lifetime.
tagline: Explore Dutch history and culture with these unforgettable day trips

title: Amsterdam Cheese Tastings
description:
Taste your way through Dutch cuisine with a cheese tasting in and around Amsterdam.
From creamy and mild to crunchy and funky, there's a cheese tasting available that will get your taste buds acquainted with the most famous food in the Netherlands!
tagline: Get to know Dutch cheese culture with a delicious cheese tasting in Amsterdam

title: Museumplein
description: This vibrant square is home to some of the city's most renowned museums and offers a captivating blend of art, history, and beauty. As you step into Museumplein, you'll be surrounded by iconic buildings and green spaces, creating a harmonious balance between architecture and nature. The square is anchored by three major museums: the Rijksmuseum, which showcases Dutch masterpieces, including Rembrandt's "The Night Watch"; the Van Gogh Museum, housing an extensive collection of the artist's works; and the Stedelijk Museum, dedicated to modern and contemporary art. The open square itself invites visitors to relax on the grass, admire the beautiful gardens, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere. Throughout the year, Museumplein hosts various cultural events, concerts, and exhibitions, making it a dynamic and lively gathering place. Immerse yourself in the rich artistic heritage of Amsterdam, explore the museums, and embrace the inspiring ambiance of Museumplein.
tagline: Where Art and Culture Flourish: Museumplein, Amsterdam

title: Rembrandtplein
tagline: Visit the famous statues and enjoy the lively nightlife

title: Amsterdam Dance Event
description:
Amsterdam Dance Event (ADE) is the most influential gathering for electronic music and its industry. With this festival, ADE is able to create a solid foundation for future generations of electronic music professionals and upcoming artists. The event attracts a total of more than 1,000 events in nearly 200 locations across its multidisciplinary program; for five days and nights.
ADE is organized by the Amsterdam Dance Event Foundation, a non-profit organization dedicated to nurturing and elevating electronic music worldwide. The organization devotes itself to stimulating development, innovation, and talent, gathering the global industry, and laying a foundation for future generations.
tagline: Come celebrate the culture of electronic music at the ADE

title: Paint and Sip Amsterdam
description:
Sip on wine, and let a local artist guide you as you paint a masterpiece. 
A relaxing and creative experience that leaves you with unique artwork and a lasting memory of your time in Amsterdam.
tagline: Get creative in the heart of Amsterdam with a brush and glass in hand

title: Portuguese Synagogue of Amsterdam
tagline: Visit the historic interior and view the grand chandeliers

title: Basiliek van de Heilige Nicolaas
tagline: Admire the grand architecture and attend a peaceful service

title: Anne Frank Monument
tagline: Learn about Anne Frank and view her statue

title: National Monument
tagline: Climb to the top and view the cityscape

title: Westertoren
tagline: Climb the tower and view the city skyline

title: NDSM Wharf
tagline: Explore street art and visit unique shops

title: The Harbour Club Theater Amsterdam
description: Attend live performances and enjoy a meal. Experience a mix of entertainment and dining.
tagline: Watch live performances and enjoy a variety of food options

title: Amsterdam Cheese Company
description: Sample a wide variety of cheeses from different regions. Purchase unique cheese-related gifts and souvenirs.
tagline: Taste cheese and watch how it's made

title: Indoor Activities in Amsterdam
description: Visit museums and view famous artworks. Relax at cozy cafes and enjoy local cuisine.
tagline: Visit the Van Gogh Museum and see the Anne Frank House

title: Hieronymus Bosch - Secret Signs
description: View a collection of artwork from a famous Dutch painter. Learn about the historical context of his pieces through informative displays.
tagline: View famous paintings and learn about the artist's life and techniques
